pub obligation_inspector: Cell<Option<ObligationInspector<'tcx>>>,\n}\n\n/// See the `error_reporting` module for more details.\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, TypeFoldable, TypeVisitable)]\npub enum ValuePairs<'tcx> {\n    Regions(ExpectedFound<ty::Region<'tcx>>),\n    Terms(ExpectedFound<ty::Term<'tcx>>),\n    Aliases(ExpectedFound<ty::AliasTerm<'tcx>>),\n    TraitRefs(ExpectedFound<ty::TraitRef<'tcx>>),\n    PolySigs(ExpectedFound<ty::PolyFnSig<'tcx>>),\n    ExistentialTraitRef(ExpectedFound<ty::PolyExistentialTraitRef<'tcx>>),\n    ExistentialProjection(ExpectedFound<ty::PolyExistentialProjection<'tcx>>),\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> ValuePairs<'tcx> {\n    pub fn ty(&self) -> Option<(Ty<'tcx>, Ty<'tcx>)> {\n        if let ValuePairs::Terms(ExpectedFound { expected, found }) = self\n            && let Some(expected) = expected.as_type()\n            && let Some(found) = found.as_type()\n        {\n            Some((expected, found))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// The trace designates the path through inference that we took to\n/// encounter an error or subtyping constraint.\n///\n/// See the `error_reporting` module for more details.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub struct TypeTrace<'tcx> {\n    pub cause: ObligationCause<'tcx>,\n    pub values: ValuePairs<'tcx>,\n}\n\n/// The origin of a `r1 <= r2` constraint.\n///\n/// See `error_reporting` module for more details\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub enum SubregionOrigin<'tcx> {\n    /// Arose from a subtyping relation\n    Subtype(Box<TypeTrace<'tcx>>),\n\n    /// When casting `&'a T` to an `&'b Trait` object,\n    /// relating `'a` to `'b`.\n    RelateObjectBound(Span),\n\n    /// Some type parameter was instantiated with the given type,\n    /// and that type must outlive some region.\n    RelateParamBound(Span, Ty<'tcx>, Option<Span>),\n\n    /// The given region parameter was instantiated with a region\n    /// that must outlive some other region.\n    RelateRegionParamBound(Span, Option<Ty<'tcx>>),\n\n    /// Creating a pointer `b` to contents of another reference.\n    Reborrow(Span),\n\n    /// (&'a &'b T) where a >= b\n    ReferenceOutlivesReferent(Ty<'tcx>, Span),\n\n    /// Comparing the signature and requirements of an impl method against\n    /// the containing trait.\n    CompareImplItemObligation {\n        span: Span,\n        impl_item_def_id: LocalDefId,\n        trait_item_def_id: DefId,\n    },\n\n    /// Checking that the bounds of a trait's associated type hold for a given impl.\n    CheckAssociatedTypeBounds {\n        parent: Box<SubregionOrigin<'tcx>>,\n        impl_item_def_id: LocalDefId,\n        trait_item_def_id: DefId,\n    },\n\n    AscribeUserTypeProvePredicate(Span),\n}\n\n// `SubregionOrigin` is used a lot. Make sure it doesn't unintentionally get bigger.\n#[cfg(target_pointer_width = \"64\")]\nrustc_data_structures::static_assert_size!(SubregionOrigin<'_>, 32);\n\nimpl<'tcx> SubregionOrigin<'tcx> {\n    pub fn to_constraint_category(&self) -> ConstraintCategory<'tcx> {\n        match self {\n            Self::Subtype(type_trace) => type_trace.cause.to_constraint_category(),\n            Self::AscribeUserTypeProvePredicate(span) => ConstraintCategory::Predicate(*span),\n            _ => ConstraintCategory::BoringNoLocation,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Times when we replace bound regions with existentials:\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]\npub enum BoundRegionConversionTime {\n    /// when a fn is called\n    FnCall,\n\n    /// when two higher-ranked types are compared\n    HigherRankedType,\n\n    /// when projecting an associated type\n    AssocTypeProjection(DefId),\n}\n\n/// Reasons to create a region inference variable.\n///\n/// See `error_reporting` module for more details.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug)]\npub enum RegionVariableOrigin {\n    /// Region variables created for ill-categorized reasons.\n    ///\n    /// They mostly indicate places in need of refactoring.\n    Misc(Span),\n\n    /// Regions created by a `&P` or `[...]` pattern.\n    PatternRegion(Span),\n\n    /// Regions created by `&` operator.\n    BorrowRegion(Span),\n\n    /// Regions created as part of an autoref of a method receiver.\n    Autoref(Span),\n\n    /// Regions created as part of an automatic coercion.\n    Coercion(Span),\n\n    /// Region variables created as the values for early-bound regions.\n    ///\n    /// FIXME(@lcnr): This should also store a `DefId`, similar to\n    /// `TypeVariableOrigin`.\n    RegionParameterDefinition(Span, Symbol),\n\n    /// Region variables created when instantiating a binder with\n    /// existential variables, e.g. when calling a function or method.\n    BoundRegion(Span, ty::BoundRegionKind, BoundRegionConversionTime),\n\n    UpvarRegion(ty::UpvarId, Span),\n\n    /// This origin is used for the inference variables that we create\n    /// during NLL region processing.\n    Nll(NllRegionVariableOrigin),\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug)]\npub enum NllRegionVariableOrigin {\n    /// During NLL region processing, we create variables for free\n    /// regions that we encounter in the function signature and\n    /// elsewhere. This origin indices we've got one of those.\n    FreeRegion,\n\n    /// \"Universal\" instantiation of a higher-ranked region (e.g.,\n    /// from a `for<'a> T` binder). Meant to represent \"any region\".\n    Placeholder(ty::PlaceholderRegion),\n\n    Existential {\n        name: Option<Symbol>,\n    },\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug)]\npub struct FixupError {\n    unresolved: TyOrConstInferVar,\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for FixupError {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self.unresolved {\n            TyOrConstInferVar::TyInt(_) => write!(\n                f,\n                \"cannot determine the type of this integer; \\\n                 add a suffix to specify the type explicitly\"\n            ),\n            TyOrConstInferVar::TyFloat(_) => write!(\n                f,\n                \"cannot determine the type of this number; \\\n                 add a suffix to specify the type explicitly\"\n            ),\n            TyOrConstInferVar::Ty(_) => write!(f, \"unconstrained type\"),\n            TyOrConstInferVar::Const(_) => write!(f, \"unconstrained const value\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// See the `region_obligations` field for more information.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub struct TypeOutlivesConstraint<'tcx> {\n    pub sub_region: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    pub sup_type: Ty<'tcx>,\n    pub origin: SubregionOrigin<'tcx>,\n}\n\n/// Used to configure inference contexts before their creation.\npub struct InferCtxtBuilder<'tcx> {\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n    considering_regions: bool,\n    in_hir_typeck: bool,\n    skip_leak_check: bool,\n    /// Whether we should use the new trait solver in the local inference context,\n    /// which affects things like which solver is used in `predicate_may_hold`.\n    next_trait_solver: bool,\n}\n\n#[extension(pub trait TyCtxtInferExt<'tcx>)]\nimpl<'tcx> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n    fn infer_ctxt(self) -> InferCtxtBuilder<'tcx> {\n        InferCtxtBuilder {\n            tcx: self,\n            considering_regions: true,\n            in_hir_typeck: false,\n            skip_leak_check: false,\n            next_trait_solver: self.next_trait_solver_globally(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> InferCtxtBuilder<'tcx> {\n    pub fn with_next_trait_solver(mut self, next_trait_solver: bool) -> Self {\n        self.next_trait_solver = next_trait_solver;\n        self\n    }\n\n    pub fn ignoring_regions(mut self) -> Self {\n        self.considering_regions = false;\n        self\n    }\n\n    pub fn in_hir_typeck(mut self) -> Self {\n        self.in_hir_typeck = true;\n        self\n    }\n\n    pub fn skip_leak_check(mut self, skip_leak_check: bool) -> Self {\n        self.skip_leak_check = skip_leak_check;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Given a canonical value `C` as a starting point, create an\n    /// inference context that contains each of the bound values\n    /// within instantiated as a fresh variable. The `f` closure is\n    /// invoked with the new infcx, along with the instantiated value\n    /// `V` and a instantiation `S`. This instantiation `S` maps from\n    /// the bound values in `C` to their instantiated values in `V`\n    /// (in other words, `S(C) = V`).\n    pub fn build_with_canonical<T>(\n        mut self,\n        span: Span,\n        input: &CanonicalQueryInput<'tcx, T>,\n    ) -> (InferCtxt<'tcx>, T, CanonicalVarValues<'tcx>)\n    where\n        T: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        let infcx = self.build(input.typing_mode);\n        let (value, args) = infcx.instantiate_canonical(span, &input.canonical);\n        (infcx, value, args)\n    }\n\n    pub fn build_with_typing_env(\n        mut self,\n        TypingEnv { typing_mode, param_env }: TypingEnv<'tcx>,\n    ) -> (InferCtxt<'tcx>, ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>) {\n        (self.build(typing_mode), param_env)\n    }\n\n    pub fn build(&mut self, typing_mode: TypingMode<'tcx>) -> InferCtxt<'tcx> {\n        let InferCtxtBuilder {\n            tcx,\n            considering_regions,\n            in_hir_typeck,\n            skip_leak_check,\n            next_trait_solver,\n        } = *self;\n        InferCtxt {\n            tcx,\n            typing_mode,\n            considering_regions,\n            in_hir_typeck,\n            skip_leak_check,\n            inner: RefCell::new(InferCtxtInner::new()),\n            lexical_region_resolutions: RefCell::new(None),\n            selection_cache: Default::default(),\n            evaluation_cache: Default::default(),\n            reported_trait_errors: Default::default(),\n            reported_signature_mismatch: Default::default(),\n            tainted_by_errors: Cell::new(None),\n            universe: Cell::new(ty::UniverseIndex::ROOT),\n            next_trait_solver,\n            obligation_inspector: Cell::new(None),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx, T> InferOk<'tcx, T> {\n    /// Extracts `value`, registering any obligations into `fulfill_cx`.\n    pub fn into_value_registering_obligations<E: 'tcx>(\n        self,\n        infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n        fulfill_cx: &mut dyn TraitEngine<'tcx, E>,\n    ) -> T {\n        let InferOk { value, obligations } = self;\n        fulfill_cx.register_predicate_obligations(infcx, obligations);\n        value\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> InferOk<'tcx, ()> {\n    pub fn into_obligations(self) -> PredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n        self.obligations\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> InferCtxt<'tcx> {\n    pub fn dcx(&self) -> DiagCtxtHandle<'_> {\n        self.tcx.dcx().taintable_handle(&self.tainted_by_errors)\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_trait_solver(&self) -> bool {\n        self.next_trait_solver\n    }\n\n    #[inline(always)]\n    pub fn typing_mode(&self) -> TypingMode<'tcx> {\n        self.typing_mode\n    }\n\n    pub fn freshen<T: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(&self, t: T) -> T {\n        t.fold_with(&mut self.freshener())\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the origin of the type variable identified by `vid`.\n    ///\n    /// No attempt is made to resolve `vid` to its root variable.\n    pub fn type_var_origin(&self, vid: TyVid) -> TypeVariableOrigin {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().var_origin(vid)\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the origin of the const variable identified by `vid`\n    // FIXME: We should store origins separately from the unification table\n    // so this doesn't need to be optional.\n    pub fn const_var_origin(&self, vid: ConstVid) -> Option<ConstVariableOrigin> {\n        match self.inner.borrow_mut().const_unification_table().probe_value(vid) {\n            ConstVariableValue::Known { .. } => None,\n            ConstVariableValue::Unknown { origin, .. } => Some(origin),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn freshener<'b>(&'b self) -> TypeFreshener<'b, 'tcx> {\n        freshen::TypeFreshener::new(self)\n    }\n\n    pub fn unresolved_variables(&self) -> Vec<Ty<'tcx>> {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.borrow_mut();\n        let mut vars: Vec<Ty<'_>> = inner\n            .type_variables()\n            .unresolved_variables()\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|t| Ty::new_var(self.tcx, t))\n            .collect();\n        vars.extend(\n            (0..inner.int_unification_table().len())\n                .map(|i| ty::IntVid::from_usize(i))\n                .filter(|&vid| inner.int_unification_table().probe_value(vid).is_unknown())\n                .map(|v| Ty::new_int_var(self.tcx, v)),\n        );\n        vars.extend(\n            (0..inner.float_unification_table().len())\n                .map(|i| ty::FloatVid::from_usize(i))\n                .filter(|&vid| inner.float_unification_table().probe_value(vid).is_unknown())\n                .map(|v| Ty::new_float_var(self.tcx, v)),\n        );\n        vars\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"debug\")]\n    pub fn sub_regions(\n        &self,\n        origin: SubregionOrigin<'tcx>,\n        a: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    ) {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().unwrap_region_constraints().make_subregion(origin, a, b);\n    }\n\n    /// Processes a `Coerce` predicate from the fulfillment context.\n    /// This is NOT the preferred way to handle coercion, which is to\n    /// invoke `FnCtxt::coerce` or a similar method (see `coercion.rs`).\n    ///\n    /// This method here is actually a fallback that winds up being\n    /// invoked when `FnCtxt::coerce` encounters unresolved type variables\n    /// and records a coercion predicate. Presently, this method is equivalent\n    /// to `subtype_predicate` -- that is, \"coercing\" `a` to `b` winds up\n    /// actually requiring `a <: b`. This is of course a valid coercion,\n    /// but it's not as flexible as `FnCtxt::coerce` would be.\n    ///\n    /// (We may refactor this in the future, but there are a number of\n    /// practical obstacles. Among other things, `FnCtxt::coerce` presently\n    /// records adjustments that are required on the HIR in order to perform\n    /// the coercion, and we don't currently have a way to manage that.)\n    pub fn coerce_predicate(\n        &self,\n        cause: &ObligationCause<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        predicate: ty::PolyCoercePredicate<'tcx>,\n    ) -> Result<InferResult<'tcx, ()>, (TyVid, TyVid)> {\n        let subtype_predicate = predicate.map_bound(|p| ty::SubtypeRelation {\n            a_is_expected: false, // when coercing from `a` to `b`, `b` is expected\n            sub_ty: p.a,\n            super_ty: p.b,\n        });\n        self.subtype_predicate(cause, param_env, subtype_predicate)\n    }\n\n    pub fn subtype_predicate(\n        &self,\n        cause: &ObligationCause<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        predicate: ty::PolySubtypePredicate<'tcx>,\n    ) -> Result<InferResult<'tcx, ()>, (TyVid, TyVid)> {\n        // Check for two unresolved inference variables, in which case we can\n        // make no progress. This is partly a micro-optimization, but it's\n        // also an opportunity to \"sub-unify\" the variables. This isn't\n        // *necessary* to prevent cycles, because they would eventually be sub-unified\n        // anyhow during generalization, but it helps with diagnostics (we can detect\n        // earlier that they are sub-unified).\n        //\n        // Note that we can just skip the binders here because\n        // type variables can't (at present, at\n        // least) capture any of the things bound by this binder.\n        //\n        // Note that this sub here is not just for diagnostics - it has semantic\n        // effects as well.\n        let r_a = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().sub_ty);\n        let r_b = self.shallow_resolve(predicate.skip_binder().super_ty);\n        match (r_a.kind(), r_b.kind()) {\n            (&ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), &ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                self.sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(a_vid, b_vid);\n                return Err((a_vid, b_vid));\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n\n        self.enter_forall(predicate, |ty::SubtypePredicate { a_is_expected, a, b }| {\n            if a_is_expected {\n                Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sub(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, a, b))\n            } else {\n                Ok(self.at(cause, param_env).sup(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, b, a))\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Number of type variables created so far.\n    pub fn num_ty_vars(&self) -> usize {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().num_vars()\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_ty_vid(&self, span: Span) -> TyVid {\n        self.next_ty_vid_with_origin(TypeVariableOrigin { span, param_def_id: None })\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_ty_vid_with_origin(&self, origin: TypeVariableOrigin) -> TyVid {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().new_var(self.universe(), origin)\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_ty_vid_in_universe(&self, span: Span, universe: ty::UniverseIndex) -> TyVid {\n        let origin = TypeVariableOrigin { span, param_def_id: None };\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().new_var(universe, origin)\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_ty_var(&self, span: Span) -> Ty<'tcx> {\n        self.next_ty_var_with_origin(TypeVariableOrigin { span, param_def_id: None })\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_ty_var_with_origin(&self, origin: TypeVariableOrigin) -> Ty<'tcx> {\n        let vid = self.next_ty_vid_with_origin(origin);\n        Ty::new_var(self.tcx, vid)\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_ty_var_in_universe(&self, span: Span, universe: ty::UniverseIndex) -> Ty<'tcx> {\n        let vid = self.next_ty_vid_in_universe(span, universe);\n        Ty::new_var(self.tcx, vid)\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_const_var(&self, span: Span) -> ty::Const<'tcx> {\n        self.next_const_var_with_origin(ConstVariableOrigin { span, param_def_id: None })\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_const_var_with_origin(&self, origin: ConstVariableOrigin) -> ty::Const<'tcx> {\n        let vid = self\n            .inner\n            .borrow_mut()\n            .const_unification_table()\n            .new_key(ConstVariableValue::Unknown { origin, universe: self.universe() })\n            .vid;\n        ty::Const::new_var(self.tcx, vid)\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_const_var_in_universe(\n        &self,\n        span: Span,\n        universe: ty::UniverseIndex,\n    ) -> ty::Const<'tcx> {\n        let origin = ConstVariableOrigin { span, param_def_id: None };\n        let vid = self\n            .inner\n            .borrow_mut()\n            .const_unification_table()\n            .new_key(ConstVariableValue::Unknown { origin, universe })\n            .vid;\n        ty::Const::new_var(self.tcx, vid)\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_int_var(&self) -> Ty<'tcx> {\n        let next_int_var_id =\n            self.inner.borrow_mut().int_unification_table().new_key(ty::IntVarValue::Unknown);\n        Ty::new_int_var(self.tcx, next_int_var_id)\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_float_var(&self) -> Ty<'tcx> {\n        let next_float_var_id =\n            self.inner.borrow_mut().float_unification_table().new_key(ty::FloatVarValue::Unknown);\n        Ty::new_float_var(self.tcx, next_float_var_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Creates a fresh region variable with the next available index.\n    /// The variable will be created in the maximum universe created\n    /// thus far, allowing it to name any region created thus far.\n    pub fn next_region_var(&self, origin: RegionVariableOrigin) -> ty::Region<'tcx> {\n        self.next_region_var_in_universe(origin, self.universe())\n    }\n\n    /// Creates a fresh region variable with the next available index\n    /// in the given universe; typically, you can use\n    /// `next_region_var` and just use the maximal universe.\n    pub fn next_region_var_in_universe(\n        &self,\n        origin: RegionVariableOrigin,\n        universe: ty::UniverseIndex,\n    ) -> ty::Region<'tcx> {\n        let region_var =\n            self.inner.borrow_mut().unwrap_region_constraints().new_region_var(universe, origin);\n        ty::Region::new_var(self.tcx, region_var)\n    }\n\n    pub fn next_term_var_of_kind(&self, term: ty::Term<'tcx>, span: Span) -> ty::Term<'tcx> {\n        match term.kind() {\n            ty::TermKind::Ty(_) => self.next_ty_var(span).into(),\n            ty::TermKind::Const(_) => self.next_const_var(span).into(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Return the universe that the region `r` was created in. For\n    /// most regions (e.g., `'static`, named regions from the user,\n    /// etc) this is the root universe U0. For inference variables or\n    /// placeholders, however, it will return the universe which they\n    /// are associated.\n    pub fn universe_of_region(&self, r: ty::Region<'tcx>) -> ty::UniverseIndex {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().unwrap_region_constraints().universe(r)\n    }\n\n    /// Number of region variables created so far.\n    pub fn num_region_vars(&self) -> usize {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().unwrap_region_constraints().num_region_vars()\n    }\n\n    /// Just a convenient wrapper of `next_region_var` for using during NLL.\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"debug\")]\n    pub fn next_nll_region_var(&self, origin: NllRegionVariableOrigin) -> ty::Region<'tcx> {\n        self.next_region_var(RegionVariableOrigin::Nll(origin))\n    }\n\n    /// Just a convenient wrapper of `next_region_var` for using during NLL.\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"debug\")]\n    pub fn next_nll_region_var_in_universe(\n        &self,\n        origin: NllRegionVariableOrigin,\n        universe: ty::UniverseIndex,\n    ) -> ty::Region<'tcx> {\n        self.next_region_var_in_universe(RegionVariableOrigin::Nll(origin), universe)\n    }\n\n    pub fn var_for_def(&self, span: Span, param: &ty::GenericParamDef) -> GenericArg<'tcx> {\n        match param.kind {\n            GenericParamDefKind::Lifetime => {\n                // Create a region inference variable for the given\n                // region parameter definition.\n                self.next_region_var(RegionVariableOrigin::RegionParameterDefinition(\n                    span, param.name,\n                ))\n                .into()\n            }\n            GenericParamDefKind::Type { .. } => {\n                // Create a type inference variable for the given\n                // type parameter definition. The generic parameters are\n                // for actual parameters that may be referred to by\n                // the default of this type parameter, if it exists.\n                // e.g., `struct Foo<A, B, C = (A, B)>(...);` when\n                // used in a path such as `Foo::<T, U>::new()` will\n                // use an inference variable for `C` with `[T, U]`\n                // as the generic parameters for the default, `(T, U)`.\n                let ty_var_id = self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().new_var(\n                    self.universe(),\n                    TypeVariableOrigin { param_def_id: Some(param.def_id), span },\n                );\n\n                Ty::new_var(self.tcx, ty_var_id).into()\n            }\n            GenericParamDefKind::Const { .. } => {\n                let origin = ConstVariableOrigin { param_def_id: Some(param.def_id), span };\n                let const_var_id = self\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .const_unification_table()\n                    .new_key(ConstVariableValue::Unknown { origin, universe: self.universe() })\n                    .vid;\n                ty::Const::new_var(self.tcx, const_var_id).into()\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Given a set of generics defined on a type or impl, returns the generic parameters mapping\n    /// each type/region parameter to a fresh inference variable.\n    pub fn fresh_args_for_item(&self, span: Span, def_id: DefId) -> GenericArgsRef<'tcx> {\n        GenericArgs::for_item(self.tcx, def_id, |param, _| self.var_for_def(span, param))\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if errors have been reported since this infcx was\n    /// created. This is sometimes used as a heuristic to skip\n    /// reporting errors that often occur as a result of earlier\n    /// errors, but where it's hard to be 100% sure (e.g., unresolved\n    /// inference variables, regionck errors).\n    #[must_use = \"this method does not have any side effects\"]\n    pub fn tainted_by_errors(&self) -> Option<ErrorGuaranteed> {\n        self.tainted_by_errors.get()\n    }\n\n    /// Set the \"tainted by errors\" flag to true. We call this when we\n    /// observe an error from a prior pass.\n    pub fn set_tainted_by_errors(&self, e: ErrorGuaranteed) {\n        debug!(\"set_tainted_by_errors(ErrorGuaranteed)\");\n        self.tainted_by_errors.set(Some(e));\n    }\n\n    pub fn region_var_origin(&self, vid: ty::RegionVid) -> RegionVariableOrigin {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.borrow_mut();\n        let inner = &mut *inner;\n        inner.unwrap_region_constraints().var_origin(vid)\n    }\n\n    /// Clone the list of variable regions. This is used only during NLL processing\n    /// to put the set of region variables into the NLL region context.\n    pub fn get_region_var_infos(&self) -> VarInfos {\n        let inner = self.inner.borrow();\n        assert!(!UndoLogs::<UndoLog<'_>>::in_snapshot(&inner.undo_log));\n        let storage = inner.region_constraint_storage.as_ref().expect(\"regions already resolved\");\n        assert!(storage.data.is_empty(), \"{:#?}\", storage.data);\n        // We clone instead of taking because borrowck still wants to use the\n        // inference context after calling this for diagnostics and the new\n        // trait solver.\n        storage.var_infos.clone()\n    }\n\n    pub fn has_opaque_types_in_storage(&self) -> bool {\n        !self.inner.borrow().opaque_type_storage.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip(self), ret)]\n    pub fn take_opaque_types(&self) -> Vec<(OpaqueTypeKey<'tcx>, ProvisionalHiddenType<'tcx>)> {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().opaque_type_storage.take_opaque_types().collect()\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip(self), ret)]\n    pub fn clone_opaque_types(&self) -> Vec<(OpaqueTypeKey<'tcx>, ProvisionalHiddenType<'tcx>)> {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().opaque_type_storage.iter_opaque_types().collect()\n    }\n\n    pub fn has_opaques_with_sub_unified_hidden_type(&self, ty_vid: TyVid) -> bool {\n        if !self.next_trait_solver() {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let ty_sub_vid = self.sub_unification_table_root_var(ty_vid);\n        let inner = &mut *self.inner.borrow_mut();\n        let mut type_variables = inner.type_variable_storage.with_log(&mut inner.undo_log);\n        inner.opaque_type_storage.iter_opaque_types().any(|(_, hidden_ty)| {\n            if let ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(hidden_vid)) = *hidden_ty.ty.kind() {\n                let opaque_sub_vid = type_variables.sub_unification_table_root_var(hidden_vid);\n                if opaque_sub_vid == ty_sub_vid {\n                    return true;\n                }\n            }\n\n            false\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Searches for an opaque type key whose hidden type is related to `ty_vid`.\n    ///\n    /// This only checks for a subtype relation, it does not require equality.\n    pub fn opaques_with_sub_unified_hidden_type(&self, ty_vid: TyVid) -> Vec<ty::AliasTy<'tcx>> {\n        // Avoid accidentally allowing more code to compile with the old solver.\n        if !self.next_trait_solver() {\n            return vec![];\n        }\n\n        let ty_sub_vid = self.sub_unification_table_root_var(ty_vid);\n        let inner = &mut *self.inner.borrow_mut();\n        // This is iffy, can't call `type_variables()` as we're already\n        // borrowing the `opaque_type_storage` here.\n        let mut type_variables = inner.type_variable_storage.with_log(&mut inner.undo_log);\n        inner\n            .opaque_type_storage\n            .iter_opaque_types()\n            .filter_map(|(key, hidden_ty)| {\n                if let ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(hidden_vid)) = *hidden_ty.ty.kind() {\n                    let opaque_sub_vid = type_variables.sub_unification_table_root_var(hidden_vid);\n                    if opaque_sub_vid == ty_sub_vid {\n                        return Some(ty::AliasTy::new_from_args(\n                            self.tcx,\n                            key.def_id.into(),\n                            key.args,\n                        ));\n                    }\n                }\n\n                None\n            })\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    #[inline(always)]\n    pub fn can_define_opaque_ty(&self, id: impl Into<DefId>) -> bool {\n        debug_assert!(!self.next_trait_solver());\n        match self.typing_mode() {\n            TypingMode::Analysis {\n                defining_opaque_types_and_generators: defining_opaque_types,\n            }\n            | TypingMode::Borrowck { defining_opaque_types } => {\n                id.into().as_local().is_some_and(|def_id| defining_opaque_types.contains(&def_id))\n            }\n            // FIXME(#132279): This function is quite weird in post-analysis\n            // and post-borrowck analysis mode. We may need to modify its uses\n            // to support PostBorrowckAnalysis in the old solver as well.\n            TypingMode::Coherence\n            | TypingMode::PostBorrowckAnalysis { .. }\n            | TypingMode::PostAnalysis => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn push_hir_typeck_potentially_region_dependent_goal(\n        &self,\n        goal: PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n    ) {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.borrow_mut();\n        inner.undo_log.push(UndoLog::PushHirTypeckPotentiallyRegionDependentGoal);\n        inner.hir_typeck_potentially_region_dependent_goals.push(goal);\n    }\n\n    pub fn take_hir_typeck_potentially_region_dependent_goals(\n        &self,\n    ) -> Vec<PredicateObligation<'tcx>> {\n        assert!(!self.in_snapshot(), \"cannot take goals in a snapshot\");\n        std::mem::take(&mut self.inner.borrow_mut().hir_typeck_potentially_region_dependent_goals)\n    }\n\n    pub fn ty_to_string(&self, t: Ty<'tcx>) -> String {\n        self.resolve_vars_if_possible(t).to_string()\n    }\n\n    /// If `TyVar(vid)` resolves to a type, return that type. Else, return the\n    /// universe index of `TyVar(vid)`.\n    pub fn probe_ty_var(&self, vid: TyVid) -> Result<Ty<'tcx>, ty::UniverseIndex> {\n        use self::type_variable::TypeVariableValue;\n\n        match self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().probe(vid) {\n            TypeVariableValue::Known { value } => Ok(value),\n            TypeVariableValue::Unknown { universe } => Err(universe),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn shallow_resolve(&self, ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> Ty<'tcx> {\n        if let ty::Infer(v) = *ty.kind() {\n            match v {\n                ty::TyVar(v) => {\n                    // Not entirely obvious: if `typ` is a type variable,\n                    // it can be resolved to an int/float variable, which\n                    // can then be recursively resolved, hence the\n                    // recursion. Note though that we prevent type\n                    // variables from unifying to other type variables\n                    // directly (though they may be embedded\n                    // structurally), and we prevent cycles in any case,\n                    // so this recursion should always be of very limited\n                    // depth.\n                    //\n                    // Note: if these two lines are combined into one we get\n                    // dynamic borrow errors on `self.inner`.\n                    let known = self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().probe(v).known();\n                    known.map_or(ty, |t| self.shallow_resolve(t))\n                }\n\n                ty::IntVar(v) => {\n                    match self.inner.borrow_mut().int_unification_table().probe_value(v) {\n                        ty::IntVarValue::IntType(ty) => Ty::new_int(self.tcx, ty),\n                        ty::IntVarValue::UintType(ty) => Ty::new_uint(self.tcx, ty),\n                        ty::IntVarValue::Unknown => ty,\n                    }\n                }\n\n                ty::FloatVar(v) => {\n                    match self.inner.borrow_mut().float_unification_table().probe_value(v) {\n                        ty::FloatVarValue::Known(ty) => Ty::new_float(self.tcx, ty),\n                        ty::FloatVarValue::Unknown => ty,\n                    }\n                }\n\n                ty::FreshTy(_) | ty::FreshIntTy(_) | ty::FreshFloatTy(_) => ty,\n            }\n        } else {\n            ty\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn shallow_resolve_const(&self, ct: ty::Const<'tcx>) -> ty::Const<'tcx> {\n        match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(infer_ct) => match infer_ct {\n                InferConst::Var(vid) => self\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .const_unification_table()\n                    .probe_value(vid)\n                    .known()\n                    .unwrap_or(ct),\n                InferConst::Fresh(_) => ct,\n            },\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_)\n            | ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _)\n            | ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_)\n            | ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(_)\n            | ty::ConstKind::Value(_)\n            | ty::ConstKind::Error(_)\n            | ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => ct,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn shallow_resolve_term(&self, term: ty::Term<'tcx>) -> ty::Term<'tcx> {\n        match term.kind() {\n            ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => self.shallow_resolve(ty).into(),\n            ty::TermKind::Const(ct) => self.shallow_resolve_const(ct).into(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn root_var(&self, var: ty::TyVid) -> ty::TyVid {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().root_var(var)\n    }\n\n    pub fn sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(&self, a: ty::TyVid, b: ty::TyVid) {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().sub_unify(a, b);\n    }\n\n    pub fn sub_unification_table_root_var(&self, var: ty::TyVid) -> ty::TyVid {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().sub_unification_table_root_var(var)\n    }\n\n    pub fn root_const_var(&self, var: ty::ConstVid) -> ty::ConstVid {\n        self.inner.borrow_mut().const_unification_table().find(var).vid\n    }\n\n    /// Resolves an int var to a rigid int type, if it was constrained to one,\n    /// or else the root int var in the unification table.\n    pub fn opportunistic_resolve_int_var(&self, vid: ty::IntVid) -> Ty<'tcx> {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.borrow_mut();\n        let value = inner.int_unification_table().probe_value(vid);\n        match value {\n            ty::IntVarValue::IntType(ty) => Ty::new_int(self.tcx, ty),\n            ty::IntVarValue::UintType(ty) => Ty::new_uint(self.tcx, ty),\n            ty::IntVarValue::Unknown => {\n                Ty::new_int_var(self.tcx, inner.int_unification_table().find(vid))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Resolves a float var to a rigid int type, if it was constrained to one,\n    /// or else the root float var in the unification table.\n    pub fn opportunistic_resolve_float_var(&self, vid: ty::FloatVid) -> Ty<'tcx> {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.borrow_mut();\n        let value = inner.float_unification_table().probe_value(vid);\n        match value {\n            ty::FloatVarValue::Known(ty) => Ty::new_float(self.tcx, ty),\n            ty::FloatVarValue::Unknown => {\n                Ty::new_float_var(self.tcx, inner.float_unification_table().find(vid))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Where possible, replaces type/const variables in\n    /// `value` with their final value. Note that region variables\n    /// are unaffected. If a type/const variable has not been unified, it\n    /// is left as is. This is an idempotent operation that does\n    /// not affect inference state in any way and so you can do it\n    /// at will.\n    pub fn resolve_vars_if_possible<T>(&self, value: T) -> T\n    where\n        T: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if let Err(guar) = value.error_reported() {\n            self.set_tainted_by_errors(guar);\n        }\n        if !value.has_non_region_infer() {\n            return value;\n        }\n        let mut r = resolve::OpportunisticVarResolver::new(self);\n        value.fold_with(&mut r)\n    }\n\n    pub fn resolve_numeric_literals_with_default<T>(&self, value: T) -> T\n    where\n        T: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if !value.has_infer() {\n            return value; // Avoid duplicated type-folding.\n        }\n        let mut r = InferenceLiteralEraser { tcx: self.tcx };\n        value.fold_with(&mut r)\n    }\n\n    pub fn probe_const_var(&self, vid: ty::ConstVid) -> Result<ty::Const<'tcx>, ty::UniverseIndex> {\n        match self.inner.borrow_mut().const_unification_table().probe_value(vid) {\n            ConstVariableValue::Known { value } => Ok(value),\n            ConstVariableValue::Unknown { origin: _, universe } => Err(universe),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Attempts to resolve all type/region/const variables in\n    /// `value`. Region inference must have been run already (e.g.,\n    /// by calling `resolve_regions_and_report_errors`). If some\n    /// variable was never unified, an `Err` results.\n    ///\n    /// This method is idempotent, but it not typically not invoked\n    /// except during the writeback phase.\n    pub fn fully_resolve<T: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(&self, value: T) -> FixupResult<T> {\n        match resolve::fully_resolve(self, value) {\n            Ok(value) => {\n                if value.has_non_region_infer() {\n                    bug!(\"`{value:?}` is not fully resolved\");\n                }\n                if value.has_infer_regions() {\n                    let guar = self.dcx().delayed_bug(format!(\"`{value:?}` is not fully resolved\"));\n                    Ok(fold_regions(self.tcx, value, |re, _| {\n                        if re.is_var() { ty::Region::new_error(self.tcx, guar) } else { re }\n                    }))\n                } else {\n                    Ok(value)\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => Err(e),\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Instantiates the bound variables in a given binder with fresh inference\n    // variables in the current universe.\n    //\n    // Use this method if you'd like to find some generic parameters of the binder's\n    // variables (e.g. during a method call). If there isn't a [`BoundRegionConversionTime`]\n    // that corresponds to your use case, consider whether or not you should\n    // use [`InferCtxt::enter_forall`] instead.\n    pub fn instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars<T>(\n        &self,\n        span: Span,\n        lbrct: BoundRegionConversionTime,\n        value: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n    ) -> T\n    where\n        T: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>> + Copy,\n    {\n        if let Some(inner) = value.no_bound_vars() {\n            return inner;\n        }\n\n        let bound_vars = value.bound_vars();\n        let mut args = Vec::with_capacity(bound_vars.len());\n\n        for bound_var_kind in bound_vars {\n            let arg: ty::GenericArg<'_> = match bound_var_kind {\n                ty::BoundVariableKind::Ty(_) => self.next_ty_var(span).into(),\n                ty::BoundVariableKind::Region(br) => {\n                    self.next_region_var(RegionVariableOrigin::BoundRegion(span, br, lbrct)).into()\n                }\n                ty::BoundVariableKind::Const => self.next_const_var(span).into(),\n            };\n            args.push(arg);\n        }\n\n        struct ToFreshVars<'tcx> {\n            args: Vec<ty::GenericArg<'tcx>>,\n        }\n\n        impl<'tcx> BoundVarReplacerDelegate<'tcx> for ToFreshVars<'tcx> {\n            fn replace_region(&mut self, br: ty::BoundRegion) -> ty::Region<'tcx> {\n                self.args[br.var.index()].expect_region()\n            }\n            fn replace_ty(&mut self, bt: ty::BoundTy) -> Ty<'tcx> {\n                self.args[bt.var.index()].expect_ty()\n            }\n            fn replace_const(&mut self, bc: ty::BoundConst) -> ty::Const<'tcx> {\n                self.args[bc.var.index()].expect_const()\n            }\n        }\n        let delegate = ToFreshVars { args };\n        self.tcx.replace_bound_vars_uncached(value, delegate)\n    }\n\n    /// See the [`region_constraints::RegionConstraintCollector::verify_generic_bound`] method.\n    pub(crate) fn verify_generic_bound(\n        &self,\n        origin: SubregionOrigin<'tcx>,\n        kind: GenericKind<'tcx>,\n        a: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n        bound: VerifyBound<'tcx>,\n    ) {\n        debug!(\"verify_generic_bound({:?}, {:?} <: {:?})\", kind, a, bound);\n\n        self.inner\n            .borrow_mut()\n            .unwrap_region_constraints()\n            .verify_generic_bound(origin, kind, a, bound);\n    }\n\n    /// Obtains the latest type of the given closure; this may be a\n    /// closure in the current function, in which case its\n    /// `ClosureKind` may not yet be known.\n    pub fn closure_kind(&self, closure_ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> Option<ty::ClosureKind> {\n        let unresolved_kind_ty = match *closure_ty.kind() {\n            ty::Closure(_, args) => args.as_closure().kind_ty(),\n            ty::CoroutineClosure(_, args) => args.as_coroutine_closure().kind_ty(),\n            _ => bug!(\"unexpected type {closure_ty}\"),\n        };\n        let closure_kind_ty = self.shallow_resolve(unresolved_kind_ty);\n        closure_kind_ty.to_opt_closure_kind()\n    }\n\n    pub fn universe(&self) -> ty::UniverseIndex {\n        self.universe.get()\n    }\n\n    /// Creates and return a fresh universe that extends all previous\n    /// universes. Updates `self.universe` to that new universe.\n    pub fn create_next_universe(&self) -> ty::UniverseIndex {\n        let u = self.universe.get().next_universe();\n        debug!(\"create_next_universe {u:?}\");\n        self.universe.set(u);\n        u\n    }\n\n    /// Extract [`ty::TypingMode`] of this inference context to get a `TypingEnv`\n    /// which contains the necessary information to use the trait system without\n    /// using canonicalization or carrying this inference context around.\n    pub fn typing_env(&self, param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>) -> ty::TypingEnv<'tcx> {\n        let typing_mode = match self.typing_mode() {\n            // FIXME(#132279): This erases the `defining_opaque_types` as it isn't possible\n            // to handle them without proper canonicalization. This means we may cause cycle\n            // errors and fail to reveal opaques while inside of bodies. We should rename this\n            // function and require explicit comments on all use-sites in the future.\n            ty::TypingMode::Analysis { defining_opaque_types_and_generators: _ }\n            | ty::TypingMode::Borrowck { defining_opaque_types: _ } => {\n                TypingMode::non_body_analysis()\n            }\n            mode @ (ty::TypingMode::Coherence\n            | ty::TypingMode::PostBorrowckAnalysis { .. }\n            | ty::TypingMode::PostAnalysis) => mode,\n        };\n        ty::TypingEnv { typing_mode, param_env }\n    }\n\n    /// Similar to [`Self::canonicalize_query`], except that it returns\n    /// a [`PseudoCanonicalInput`] and requires both the `value` and the\n    /// `param_env` to not contain any inference variables or placeholders.\n    pub fn pseudo_canonicalize_query<V>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        value: V,\n    ) -> PseudoCanonicalInput<'tcx, V>\n    where\n        V: TypeVisitable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        debug_assert!(!value.has_infer());\n        debug_assert!(!value.has_placeholders());\n        debug_assert!(!param_env.has_infer());\n        debug_assert!(!param_env.has_placeholders());\n        self.typing_env(param_env).as_query_input(value)\n    }\n\n    /// The returned function is used in a fast path. If it returns `true` the variable is\n    /// unchanged, `false` indicates that the status is unknown.\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_ty_infer_var_definitely_unchanged(&self) -> impl Fn(TyOrConstInferVar) -> bool {\n        // This hoists the borrow/release out of the loop body.\n        let inner = self.inner.try_borrow();\n\n        move |infer_var: TyOrConstInferVar| match (infer_var, &inner) {\n            (TyOrConstInferVar::Ty(ty_var), Ok(inner)) => {\n                use self::type_variable::TypeVariableValue;\n\n                matches!(\n                    inner.try_type_variables_probe_ref(ty_var),\n                    Some(TypeVariableValue::Unknown { .. })\n                )\n            }\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// `ty_or_const_infer_var_changed` is equivalent to one of these two:\n    ///   * `shallow_resolve(ty) != ty` (where `ty.kind = ty::Infer(_)`)\n    ///   * `shallow_resolve(ct) != ct` (where `ct.kind = ty::ConstKind::Infer(_)`)\n    ///\n    /// However, `ty_or_const_infer_var_changed` is more efficient. It's always\n    /// inlined, despite being large, because it has only two call sites that\n    /// are extremely hot (both in `traits::fulfill`'s checking of `stalled_on`\n    /// inference variables), and it handles both `Ty` and `ty::Const` without\n    /// having to resort to storing full `GenericArg`s in `stalled_on`.\n    #[inline(always)]\n    pub fn ty_or_const_infer_var_changed(&self, infer_var: TyOrConstInferVar) -> bool {\n        match infer_var {\n            TyOrConstInferVar::Ty(v) => {\n                use self::type_variable::TypeVariableValue;\n\n                // If `inlined_probe` returns a `Known` value, it never equals\n                // `ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(v))`.\n                match self.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().inlined_probe(v) {\n                    TypeVariableValue::Unknown { .. } => false,\n                    TypeVariableValue::Known { .. } => true,\n                }\n            }\n\n            TyOrConstInferVar::TyInt(v) => {\n                // If `inlined_probe_value` returns a value it's always a\n                // `ty::Int(_)` or `ty::UInt(_)`, which never matches a\n                // `ty::Infer(_)`.\n                self.inner.borrow_mut().int_unification_table().inlined_probe_value(v).is_known()\n            }\n\n            TyOrConstInferVar::TyFloat(v) => {\n                // If `probe_value` returns a value it's always a\n                // `ty::Float(_)`, which never matches a `ty::Infer(_)`.\n                //\n                // Not `inlined_probe_value(v)` because this call site is colder.\n                self.inner.borrow_mut().float_unification_table().probe_value(v).is_known()\n            }\n\n            TyOrConstInferVar::Const(v) => {\n                // If `probe_value` returns a `Known` value, it never equals\n                // `ty::ConstKind::Infer(ty::InferConst::Var(v))`.\n                //\n                // Not `inlined_probe_value(v)` because this call site is colder.\n                match self.inner.borrow_mut().const_unification_table().probe_value(v) {\n                    ConstVariableValue::Unknown { .. } => false,\n                    ConstVariableValue::Known { .. } => true,\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Attach a callback to be invoked on each root obligation evaluated in the new trait solver.\n    pub fn attach_obligation_inspector(&self, inspector: ObligationInspector<'tcx>) {\n        debug_assert!(\n            self.obligation_inspector.get().is_none(),\n            \"shouldn't override a set obligation inspector\"\n        );\n        self.obligation_inspector.set(Some(inspector));\n    }\n}\n\n/// Helper for [InferCtxt::ty_or_const_infer_var_changed] (see comment on that), currently\n/// used only for `traits::fulfill`'s list of `stalled_on` inference variables.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug)]\npub enum TyOrConstInferVar {\n    /// Equivalent to `ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(_))`.\n    Ty(TyVid),\n    /// Equivalent to `ty::Infer(ty::IntVar(_))`.\n    TyInt(IntVid),\n    /// Equivalent to `ty::Infer(ty::FloatVar(_))`.\n    TyFloat(FloatVid),\n\n    /// Equivalent to `ty::ConstKind::Infer(ty::InferConst::Var(_))`.\n    Const(ConstVid),\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TyOrConstInferVar {\n    /// Tries to extract an inference variable from a type or a constant, returns `None`\n    /// for types other than `ty::Infer(_)` (or `InferTy::Fresh*`) and\n    /// for constants other than `ty::ConstKind::Infer(_)` (or `InferConst::Fresh`).\n    pub fn maybe_from_generic_arg(arg: GenericArg<'tcx>) -> Option<Self> {\n        match arg.kind() {\n            GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => Self::maybe_from_ty(ty),\n            GenericArgKind::Const(ct) => Self::maybe_from_const(ct),\n            GenericArgKind::Lifetime(_) => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Tries to extract an inference variable from a type or a constant, returns `None`\n    /// for types other than `ty::Infer(_)` (or `InferTy::Fresh*`) and\n    /// for constants other than `ty::ConstKind::Infer(_)` (or `InferConst::Fresh`).\n    pub fn maybe_from_term(term: Term<'tcx>) -> Option<Self> {\n        match term.kind() {\n            TermKind::Ty(ty) => Self::maybe_from_ty(ty),\n            TermKind::Const(ct) => Self::maybe_from_const(ct),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Tries to extract an inference variable from a type, returns `None`\n    /// for types other than `ty::Infer(_)` (or `InferTy::Fresh*`).\n    fn maybe_from_ty(ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> Option<Self> {\n        match *ty.kind() {\n            ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(v)) => Some(TyOrConstInferVar::Ty(v)),\n            ty::Infer(ty::IntVar(v)) => Some(TyOrConstInferVar::TyInt(v)),\n            ty::Infer(ty::FloatVar(v)) => Some(TyOrConstInferVar::TyFloat(v)),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Tries to extract an inference variable from a constant, returns `None`\n    /// for constants other than `ty::ConstKind::Infer(_)` (or `InferConst::Fresh`).\n    fn maybe_from_const(ct: ty::Const<'tcx>) -> Option<Self> {\n        match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(InferConst::Var(v)) => Some(TyOrConstInferVar::Const(v)),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Replace `{integer}` with `i32` and `{float}` with `f64`.\n/// Used only for diagnostics.\nstruct InferenceLiteralEraser<'tcx> {\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeFolder<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for InferenceLiteralEraser<'tcx> {\n    fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n        self.tcx\n    }\n\n    fn fold_ty(&mut self, ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> Ty<'tcx> {\n        match ty.kind() {\n            ty::Infer(ty::IntVar(_) | ty::FreshIntTy(_)) => self.tcx.types.i32,\n            ty::Infer(ty::FloatVar(_) | ty::FreshFloatTy(_)) => self.tcx.types.f64,\n            _ => ty.super_fold_with(self),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeTrace<'tcx> {\n    pub fn span(&self) -> Span {\n        self.cause.span\n    }\n\n    pub fn types(cause: &ObligationCause<'tcx>, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) -> TypeTrace<'tcx> {\n        TypeTrace {\n            cause: cause.clone(),\n            values: ValuePairs::Terms(ExpectedFound::new(a.into(), b.into())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn trait_refs(\n        cause: &ObligationCause<'tcx>,\n        a: ty::TraitRef<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::TraitRef<'tcx>,\n    ) -> TypeTrace<'tcx> {\n        TypeTrace { cause: cause.clone(), values: ValuePairs::TraitRefs(ExpectedFound::new(a, b)) }\n    }\n\n    pub fn consts(\n        cause: &ObligationCause<'tcx>,\n        a: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    ) -> TypeTrace<'tcx> {\n        TypeTrace {\n            cause: cause.clone(),\n            values: ValuePairs::Terms(ExpectedFound::new(a.into(), b.into())),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> SubregionOrigin<'tcx> {\n    pub fn span(&self) -> Span {\n        match *self {\n            SubregionOrigin::Subtype(ref a) => a.span(),\n            SubregionOrigin::RelateObjectBound(a) => a,\n            SubregionOrigin::RelateParamBound(a, ..) => a,\n            SubregionOrigin::RelateRegionParamBound(a, _) => a,\n            SubregionOrigin::Reborrow(a) => a,\n            SubregionOrigin::ReferenceOutlivesReferent(_, a) => a,\n            SubregionOrigin::CompareImplItemObligation { span, .. } => span,\n            SubregionOrigin::AscribeUserTypeProvePredicate(span) => span,\n            SubregionOrigin::CheckAssociatedTypeBounds { ref parent, .. } => parent.span(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn from_obligation_cause<F>(cause: &traits::ObligationCause<'tcx>, default: F) -> Self\n    where\n        F: FnOnce() -> Self,\n    {\n        match *cause.code() {\n            traits::ObligationCauseCode::ReferenceOutlivesReferent(ref_type) => {\n                SubregionOrigin::ReferenceOutlivesReferent(ref_type, cause.span)\n            }\n\n            traits::ObligationCauseCode::CompareImplItem {\n                impl_item_def_id,\n                trait_item_def_id,\n                kind: _,\n            } => SubregionOrigin::CompareImplItemObligation {\n                span: cause.span,\n                impl_item_def_id,\n                trait_item_def_id,\n            },\n\n            traits::ObligationCauseCode::CheckAssociatedTypeBounds {\n                impl_item_def_id,\n                trait_item_def_id,\n            } => SubregionOrigin::CheckAssociatedTypeBounds {\n                impl_item_def_id,\n                trait_item_def_id,\n                parent: Box::new(default()),\n            },\n\n            traits::ObligationCauseCode::AscribeUserTypeProvePredicate(span) => {\n                SubregionOrigin::AscribeUserTypeProvePredicate(span)\n            }\n\n The next-generation trait solver, currently still WIP.\n//!\n//! As a user of rust, you can use `-Znext-solver` to enable the new trait solver.\n//!\n//! As a developer of rustc, you shouldn't be using the new trait\n//! solver without asking the trait-system-refactor-initiative, but it can\n//! be enabled with `InferCtxtBuilder::with_next_trait_solver`. This will\n//! ensure that trait solving using that inference context will be routed\n//! to the new trait solver.\n//!\n//! For a high-level overview of how this solver works, check out the relevant\n//! section of the rustc-dev-guide.\n\nmod alias_relate;\nmod assembly;\nmod effect_goals;\nmod eval_ctxt;\npub mod inspect;\nmod normalizes_to;\nmod project_goals;\nmod search_graph;\nmod trait_goals;\n\nuse derive_where::derive_where;\nuse rustc_type_ir::inherent::*;\npub use rustc_type_ir::solve::*;\nuse rustc_type_ir::{self as ty, Interner, TyVid, TypingMode};\nuse tracing::instrument;\n\npub use self::eval_ctxt::{\n    EvalCtxt, GenerateProofTree, SolverDelegateEvalExt,\n    evaluate_root_goal_for_proof_tree_raw_provider,\n};\nuse crate::delegate::SolverDelegate;\nuse crate::solve::assembly::Candidate;\n\n/// How many fixpoint iterations we should attempt inside of the solver before bailing\n/// with overflow.\n///\n/// We previously used  `cx.recursion_limit().0.checked_ilog2().unwrap_or(0)` for this.\n/// However, it feels unlikely that uncreasing the recursion limit by a power of two\n/// to get one more iteration is every useful or desirable. We now instead used a constant\n/// here. If there ever ends up some use-cases where a bigger number of fixpoint iterations\n/// is required, we can add a new attribute for that or revert this to be dependant on the\n/// recursion limit again. However, this feels very unlikely.\nconst FIXPOINT_STEP_LIMIT: usize = 8;\n\n/// Whether evaluating this goal ended up changing the\n/// inference state.\n#[derive(PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Clone, Copy)]\npub enum HasChanged {\n    Yes,\n    No,\n}\n\n// FIXME(trait-system-refactor-initiative#117): we don't detect whether a response\n// ended up pulling down any universes.\nfn has_no_inference_or_external_constraints<I: Interner>(\n    response: ty::Canonical<I, Response<I>>,\n) -> bool {\n    let ExternalConstraintsData {\n        ref region_constraints,\n        ref opaque_types,\n        ref normalization_nested_goals,\n    } = *response.value.external_constraints;\n    response.value.var_values.is_identity()\n        && region_constraints.is_empty()\n        && opaque_types.is_empty()\n        && normalization_nested_goals.is_empty()\n}\n\nfn has_only_region_constraints<I: Interner>(response: ty::Canonical<I, Response<I>>) -> bool {\n    let ExternalConstraintsData {\n        region_constraints: _,\n        ref opaque_types,\n        ref normalization_nested_goals,\n    } = *response.value.external_constraints;\n    response.value.var_values.is_identity_modulo_regions()\n        && opaque_types.is_empty()\n        && normalization_nested_goals.is_empty()\n}\n\nimpl<'a, D, I> EvalCtxt<'a, D>\nwhere\n    D: SolverDelegate<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_type_outlives_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        goal: Goal<I, ty::OutlivesPredicate<I, I::Ty>>,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        let ty::OutlivesPredicate(ty, lt) = goal.predicate;\n        self.register_ty_outlives(ty, lt);\n        self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_region_outlives_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        goal: Goal<I, ty::OutlivesPredicate<I, I::Region>>,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        let ty::OutlivesPredicate(a, b) = goal.predicate;\n        self.register_region_outlives(a, b);\n        self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_coerce_goal(&mut self, goal: Goal<I, ty::CoercePredicate<I>>) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        self.compute_subtype_goal(Goal {\n            param_env: goal.param_env,\n            predicate: ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                a_is_expected: false,\n                a: goal.predicate.a,\n                b: goal.predicate.b,\n            },\n        })\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_subtype_goal(&mut self, goal: Goal<I, ty::SubtypeRelation<I>>) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        match (goal.predicate.a.kind(), goal.predicate.b.kind()) {\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                self.sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(a_vid, b_vid);\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n            }\n            _ => {\n                self.sub(goal.param_env, goal.predicate.a, goal.predicate.b)?;\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn compute_dyn_compatible_goal(&mut self, trait_def_id: I::TraitId) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        if self.cx().trait_is_dyn_compatible(trait_def_id) {\n            self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n        } else {\n            Err(NoSolution)\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_well_formed_goal(&mut self, goal: Goal<I, I::Term>) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        match self.well_formed_goals(goal.param_env, goal.predicate) {\n            Some(goals) => {\n                self.add_goals(GoalSource::Misc, goals);\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            None => self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn compute_unstable_feature_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: <I as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        symbol: <I as Interner>::Symbol,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        if self.may_use_unstable_feature(param_env, symbol) {\n            self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n        } else {\n            self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Maybe {\n                cause: MaybeCause::Ambiguity,\n                opaque_types_jank: OpaqueTypesJank::AllGood,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_const_evaluatable_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        Goal { param_env, predicate: ct }: Goal<I, I::Const>,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n                // We never return `NoSolution` here as `evaluate_const` emits an\n                // error itself when failing to evaluate, so emitting an additional fulfillment\n                // error in that case is unnecessary noise. This may change in the future once\n                // evaluation failures are allowed to impact selection, e.g. generic const\n                // expressions in impl headers or `where`-clauses.\n\n                // FIXME(generic_const_exprs): Implement handling for generic\n                // const expressions here.\n                if let Some(_normalized) = self.evaluate_const(param_env, uv) {\n                    self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n                } else {\n                    self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n                }\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => {\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) | ty::ConstKind::Value(_) | ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => {\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            // We can freely ICE here as:\n            // - `Param` gets replaced with a placeholder during canonicalization\n            // - `Bound` cannot exist as we don't have a binder around the self Type\n            // - `Expr` is part of `feature(generic_const_exprs)` and is not implemented yet\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_) | ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) | ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => {\n                panic!(\"unexpected const kind: {:?}\", ct)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn compute_const_arg_has_type_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        goal: Goal<I, (I::Const, I::Ty)>,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        let (ct, ty) = goal.predicate;\n        let ct = self.structurally_normalize_const(goal.param_env, ct)?;\n\n        let ct_ty = match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => {\n                return self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => {\n                return self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n                self.cx().type_of(uv.def.into()).instantiate(self.cx(), uv.args)\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => unimplemented!(\n                \"`feature(generic_const_exprs)` is not supported in the new trait solver\"\n            ),\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_) => {\n                unreachable!(\"`ConstKind::Param` should have been canonicalized to `Placeholder`\")\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) => panic!(\"escaping bound vars in {:?}\", ct),\n            ty::ConstKind::Value(cv) => cv.ty(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(placeholder) => {\n                placeholder.find_const_ty_from_env(goal.param_env)\n            }\n        };\n\n        self.eq(goal.param_env, ct_ty, ty)?;\n        self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\nenum MergeCandidateInfo {\n    AlwaysApplicable(usize),\n    EqualResponse,\n}\n\nimpl<D, I> EvalCtxt<'_, D>\nwhere\n    D: SolverDelegate<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    /// Try to merge multiple possible ways to prove a goal, if that is not possible returns `None`.\n    ///\n    /// In this case we tend to flounder and return ambiguity by calling `[EvalCtxt::flounder]`.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn try_merge_candidates(\n        &mut self,\n        candidates: &[Candidate<I>],\n    ) -> Option<(CanonicalResponse<I>, MergeCandidateInfo)> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let always_applicable = candidates.iter().enumerate().find(|(_, candidate)| {\n            candidate.result.value.certainty == Certainty::Yes\n                && has_no_inference_or_external_constraints(candidate.result)\n        });\n        if let Some((i, c)) = always_applicable {\n            return Some((c.result, MergeCandidateInfo::AlwaysApplicable(i)));\n        }\n\n        let one: CanonicalResponse<I> = candidates[0].result;\n        if candidates[1..].iter().all(|candidate| candidate.result == one) {\n            return Some((one, MergeCandidateInfo::EqualResponse));\n        }\n\n        None\n    }\n\n    fn bail_with_ambiguity(&mut self, candidates: &[Candidate<I>]) -> CanonicalResponse<I> {\n        debug_assert!(candidates.len() > 1);\n        let (cause, opaque_types_jank) = candidates.iter().fold(\n            (MaybeCause::Ambiguity, OpaqueTypesJank::AllGood),\n            |(c, jank), candidates| {\n                // We pull down the certainty of `Certainty::Yes` to ambiguity when combining\n                // these responses, b/c we're combining more than one response and this we\n                // don't know which one applies.\n                match candidates.result.value.certainty {\n                    Certainty::Yes => (c, jank),\n                    Certainty::Maybe { cause, opaque_types_jank } => {\n                        (c.or(cause), jank.or(opaque_types_jank))\n                    }\n                }\n            },\n        );\n        self.make_ambiguous_response_no_constraints(cause, opaque_types_jank)\n    }\n\n    /// If we fail to merge responses we flounder and return overflow or ambiguity.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn flounder(&mut self, candidates: &[Candidate<I>]) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return Err(NoSolution);\n        } else {\n            Ok(self.bail_with_ambiguity(candidates))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a type for when it is structurally matched on.\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before matching on a type.\n    /// Not doing so is likely to be incomplete and therefore unsound during\n    /// coherence.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self, param_env), ret)]\n    fn structurally_normalize_ty(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        ty: I::Ty,\n    ) -> Result<I::Ty, NoSolution> {\n        self.structurally_normalize_term(param_env, ty.into()).map(|term| term.expect_ty())\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a const for when it is structurally matched on, or more likely\n    /// when it needs `.try_to_*` called on it (e.g. to turn it into a usize).\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before matching on a const.\n    /// Not doing so is likely to be incomplete and therefore unsound during\n    /// coherence.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self, param_env), ret)]\n    fn structurally_normalize_const(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        ct: I::Const,\n    ) -> Result<I::Const, NoSolution> {\n        self.structurally_normalize_term(param_env, ct.into()).map(|term| term.expect_const())\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a term for when it is structurally matched on.\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before matching on a ty/const.\n    /// Not doing so is likely to be incomplete and therefore unsound during coherence.\n    fn structurally_normalize_term(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        term: I::Term,\n    ) -> Result<I::Term, NoSolution> {\n        if let Some(_) = term.to_alias_term() {\n            let normalized_term = self.next_term_infer_of_kind(term);\n            let alias_relate_goal = Goal::new(\n                self.cx(),\n                param_env,\n                ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                    term,\n                    normalized_term,\n                    ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n                ),\n            );\n            // We normalize the self type to be able to relate it with\n            // types from candidates.\n            self.add_goal(GoalSource::TypeRelating, alias_relate_goal);\n            self.try_evaluate_added_goals()?;\n            Ok(self.resolve_vars_if_possible(normalized_term))\n        } else {\n            Ok(term)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn opaque_type_is_rigid(&self, def_id: I::DefId) -> bool {\n        match self.typing_mode() {\n            // Opaques are never rigid outside of analysis mode.\n            TypingMode::Coherence | TypingMode::PostAnalysis => false,\n            // During analysis, opaques are rigid unless they may be defined by\n            // the current body.\n            TypingMode::Analysis { defining_opaque_types_and_generators: non_rigid_opaques }\n            | TypingMode::Borrowck { defining_opaque_types: non_rigid_opaques }\n            | TypingMode::PostBorrowckAnalysis { defined_opaque_types: non_rigid_opaques } => {\n                !def_id.as_local().is_some_and(|def_id| non_rigid_opaques.contains(&def_id))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// The result of evaluating a goal.\n#[derive_where(Debug; I: Interner)]\npub struct GoalEvaluation<I: Interner> {\n    /// The goal we've evaluated. This is the input goal, but potentially with its\n    /// inference variables resolved. This never applies any inference constraints\n    /// from evaluating the goal.\n    ///\n    /// We rely on this to check whether root goals in HIR typeck had an unresolved\n    /// type inference variable in the input. We must not resolve this after evaluating\n    /// the goal as even if the inference variable has been resolved by evaluating the\n    /// goal itself, this goal may still end up failing due to region uniquification\n    /// later on.\n    ///\n    /// This is used as a minor optimization to avoid re-resolving inference variables\n    /// when reevaluating ambiguous goals. E.g. if we've got a goal `?x: Trait` with `?x`\n    /// already being constrained to `Vec<?y>`, then the first evaluation resolves it to\n    /// `Vec<?y>: Trait`. If this goal is still ambiguous and we later resolve `?y` to `u32`,\n    /// then reevaluating this goal now only needs to resolve `?y` while it would otherwise\n    /// have to resolve both `?x` and `?y`,\n    pub goal: Goal<I, I::Predicate>,\n    pub certainty: Certainty,\n    pub has_changed: HasChanged,\n    /// If the [`Certainty`] was `Maybe`, then keep track of whether the goal has changed\n    /// before rerunning it.\n    pub stalled_on: Option<GoalStalledOn<I>>,\n}\n\n/// The conditions that must change for a goal to warrant\n#[derive_where(Clone, Debug; I: Interner)]\npub struct GoalStalledOn<I: Interner> {\n    pub num_opaques: usize,\n    pub stalled_vars: Vec<I::GenericArg>,\n    pub sub_roots: Vec<TyVid>,\n    /// The certainty that will be returned on subsequent evaluations if this\n    /// goal remains stalled.\n    pub stalled_certainty: Certainty,\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":111,"oldLines":8,"newStart":111,"newLines":8,"lines":["             param_env: goal.param_env,","             predicate: ty::SubtypeRelation {","                 a_is_expected: false,","-                a: goal.predicate.a,","-                b: goal.predicate.b,","+                sub_ty: goal.predicate.a,","+                super_ty: goal.predicate.b,","             },","         })","     }"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

The next-generation trait solver, currently still WIP.\n//!\n//! As a user of rust, you can use `-Znext-solver` to enable the new trait solver.\n//!\n//! As a developer of rustc, you shouldn't be using the new trait\n//! solver without asking the trait-system-refactor-initiative, but it can\n//! be enabled with `InferCtxtBuilder::with_next_trait_solver`. This will\n//! ensure that trait solving using that inference context will be routed\n//! to the new trait solver.\n//!\n//! For a high-level overview of how this solver works, check out the relevant\n//! section of the rustc-dev-guide.\n\nmod alias_relate;\nmod assembly;\nmod effect_goals;\nmod eval_ctxt;\npub mod inspect;\nmod normalizes_to;\nmod project_goals;\nmod search_graph;\nmod trait_goals;\n\nuse derive_where::derive_where;\nuse rustc_type_ir::inherent::*;\npub use rustc_type_ir::solve::*;\nuse rustc_type_ir::{self as ty, Interner, TyVid, TypingMode};\nuse tracing::instrument;\n\npub use self::eval_ctxt::{\n    EvalCtxt, GenerateProofTree, SolverDelegateEvalExt,\n    evaluate_root_goal_for_proof_tree_raw_provider,\n};\nuse crate::delegate::SolverDelegate;\nuse crate::solve::assembly::Candidate;\n\n/// How many fixpoint iterations we should attempt inside of the solver before bailing\n/// with overflow.\n///\n/// We previously used  `cx.recursion_limit().0.checked_ilog2().unwrap_or(0)` for this.\n/// However, it feels unlikely that uncreasing the recursion limit by a power of two\n/// to get one more iteration is every useful or desirable. We now instead used a constant\n/// here. If there ever ends up some use-cases where a bigger number of fixpoint iterations\n/// is required, we can add a new attribute for that or revert this to be dependant on the\n/// recursion limit again.
