 fn compute_subtype_goal(&mut self, goal: Goal<I, ty::SubtypeRelation<I>>) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        match (goal.predicate.a.kind(), goal.predicate.b.kind()) {\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                self.sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(a_vid, b_vid);\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n            }\n            _ => {\n                self.sub(goal.param_env, goal.predicate.a, goal.predicate.b)?;\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn compute_dyn_compatible_goal(&mut self, trait_def_id: I::TraitId) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        if self.cx().trait_is_dyn_compatible(trait_def_id) {\n            self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n        } else {\n            Err(NoSolution)\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_well_formed_goal(&mut self, goal: Goal<I, I::Term>) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        match self.well_formed_goals(goal.param_env, goal.predicate) {\n            Some(goals) => {\n                self.add_goals(GoalSource::Misc, goals);\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            None => self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn compute_unstable_feature_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: <I as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        symbol: <I as Interner>::Symbol,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        if self.may_use_unstable_feature(param_env, symbol) {\n            self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n        } else {\n            self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Maybe {\n                cause: MaybeCause::Ambiguity,\n                opaque_types_jank: OpaqueTypesJank::AllGood,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]\n    fn compute_const_evaluatable_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        Goal { param_env, predicate: ct }: Goal<I, I::Const>,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n                // We never return `NoSolution` here as `evaluate_const` emits an\n                // error itself when failing to evaluate, so emitting an additional fulfillment\n                // error in that case is unnecessary noise. This may change in the future once\n                // evaluation failures are allowed to impact selection, e.g. generic const\n                // expressions in impl headers or `where`-clauses.\n\n                // FIXME(generic_const_exprs): Implement handling for generic\n                // const expressions here.\n                if let Some(_normalized) = self.evaluate_const(param_env, uv) {\n                    self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n                } else {\n                    self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n                }\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => {\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) | ty::ConstKind::Value(_) | ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => {\n                self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            // We can freely ICE here as:\n            // - `Param` gets replaced with a placeholder during canonicalization\n            // - `Bound` cannot exist as we don't have a binder around the self Type\n            // - `Expr` is part of `feature(generic_const_exprs)` and is not implemented yet\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_) | ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) | ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => {\n                panic!(\"unexpected const kind: {:?}\", ct)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn compute_const_arg_has_type_goal(\n        &mut self,\n        goal: Goal<I, (I::Const, I::Ty)>,\n    ) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        let (ct, ty) = goal.predicate;\n        let ct = self.structurally_normalize_const(goal.param_env, ct)?;\n\n        let ct_ty = match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => {\n                return self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => {\n                return self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n                self.cx().type_of(uv.def.into()).instantiate(self.cx(), uv.args)\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => unimplemented!(\n                \"`feature(generic_const_exprs)` is not supported in the new trait solver\"\n            ),\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_) => {\n                unreachable!(\"`ConstKind::Param` should have been canonicalized to `Placeholder`\")\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) => panic!(\"escaping bound vars in {:?}\", ct),\n            ty::ConstKind::Value(cv) => cv.ty(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(placeholder) => {\n                placeholder.find_const_ty_from_env(goal.param_env)\n            }\n        };\n\n        self.eq(goal.param_env, ct_ty, ty)?;\n        self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\nenum MergeCandidateInfo {\n    AlwaysApplicable(usize),\n    EqualResponse,\n}\n\nimpl<D, I> EvalCtxt<'_, D>\nwhere\n    D: SolverDelegate<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    /// Try to merge multiple possible ways to prove a goal, if that is not possible returns `None`.\n    ///\n    /// In this case we tend to flounder and return ambiguity by calling `[EvalCtxt::flounder]`.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn try_merge_candidates(\n        &mut self,\n        candidates: &[Candidate<I>],\n    ) -> Option<(CanonicalResponse<I>, MergeCandidateInfo)> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let always_applicable = candidates.iter().enumerate().find(|(_, candidate)| {\n            candidate.result.value.certainty == Certainty::Yes\n                && has_no_inference_or_external_constraints(candidate.result)\n        });\n        if let Some((i, c)) = always_applicable {\n            return Some((c.result, MergeCandidateInfo::AlwaysApplicable(i)));\n        }\n\n        let one: CanonicalResponse<I> = candidates[0].result;\n        if candidates[1..].iter().all(|candidate| candidate.result == one) {\n            return Some((one, MergeCandidateInfo::EqualResponse));\n        }\n\n        None\n    }\n\n    fn bail_with_ambiguity(&mut self, candidates: &[Candidate<I>]) -> CanonicalResponse<I> {\n        debug_assert!(candidates.len() > 1);\n        let (cause, opaque_types_jank) = candidates.iter().fold(\n            (MaybeCause::Ambiguity, OpaqueTypesJank::AllGood),\n            |(c, jank), candidates| {\n                // We pull down the certainty of `Certainty::Yes` to ambiguity when combining\n                // these responses, b/c we're combining more than one response and this we\n                // don't know which one applies.\n                match candidates.result.value.certainty {\n                    Certainty::Yes => (c, jank),\n                    Certainty::Maybe { cause, opaque_types_jank } => {\n                        (c.or(cause), jank.or(opaque_types_jank))\n                    }\n                }\n            },\n        );\n        self.make_ambiguous_response_no_constraints(cause, opaque_types_jank)\n    }\n\n    /// If we fail to merge responses we flounder and return overflow or ambiguity.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self), ret)]\n    fn flounder(&mut self, candidates: &[Candidate<I>]) -> QueryResult<I> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return Err(NoSolution);\n        } else {\n            Ok(self.bail_with_ambiguity(candidates))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a type for when it is structurally matched on.\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before matching on a type.\n    /// Not doing so is likely to be incomplete and therefore unsound during\n    /// coherence.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self, param_env), ret)]\n    fn structurally_normalize_ty(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        ty: I::Ty,\n    ) -> Result<I::Ty, NoSolution> {\n        self.structurally_normalize_term(param_env, ty.into()).map(|term| term.expect_ty())\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a const for when it is structurally matched on, or more likely\n    /// when it needs `.try_to_*` called on it (e.g. to turn it into a usize).\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before matching on a const.\n    /// Not doing so is likely to be incomplete and therefore unsound during\n    /// coherence.\n    #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self, param_env), ret)]\n    fn structurally_normalize_const(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        ct: I::Const,\n    ) -> Result<I::Const, NoSolution> {\n        self.structurally_normalize_term(param_env, ct.into()).map(|term| term.expect_const())\n    }\n\n    /// Normalize a term for when it is structurally matched on.\n    ///\n    /// This function is necessary in nearly all cases before matching on a ty/const.\n    /// Not doing so is likely to be incomplete and therefore unsound during coherence.\n    fn structurally_normalize_term(\n        &mut self,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        term: I::Term,\n    ) -> Result<I::Term, NoSolution> {\n        if let Some(_) = term.to_alias_term() {\n            let normalized_term = self.next_term_infer_of_kind(term);\n            let alias_relate_goal = Goal::new(\n                self.cx(),\n                param_env,\n                ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                    term,\n                    normalized_term,\n                    ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n                ),\n            );\n            // We normalize the self type to be able to relate it with\n            // types from candidates.\n            self.add_goal(GoalSource::TypeRelating, alias_relate_goal);\n            self.try_evaluate_added_goals()?;\n            Ok(self.resolve_vars_if_possible(normalized_term))\n        } else {\n            Ok(term)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn opaque_type_is_rigid(&self, def_id: I::DefId) -> bool {\n        match self.typing_mode() {\n            // Opaques are never rigid outside of analysis mode.\n            TypingMode::Coherence | TypingMode::PostAnalysis => false,\n            // During analysis, opaques are rigid unless they may be defined by\n            // the current body.\n            TypingMode::Analysis { defining_opaque_types_and_generators: non_rigid_opaques }\n            | TypingMode::Borrowck { defining_opaque_types: non_rigid_opaques }\n            | TypingMode::PostBorrowckAnalysis { defined_opaque_types: non_rigid_opaques } => {\n                !def_id.as_local().is_some_and(|def_id| non_rigid_opaques.contains(&def_id))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// The result of evaluating a goal.\n#[derive_where(Debug; I: Interner)]\npub struct GoalEvaluation<I: Interner> {\n    /// The goal we've evaluated. This is the input goal, but potentially with its\n    /// inference variables resolved. This never applies any inference constraints\n    /// from evaluating the goal.\n    ///\n    /// We rely on this to check whether root goals in HIR typeck had an unresolved\n    /// type inference variable in the input. We must not resolve this after evaluating\n    /// the goal as even if the inference variable has been resolved by evaluating the\n    /// goal itself, this goal may still end up failing due to region uniquification\n    /// later on.\n    ///\n    /// This is used as a minor optimization to avoid re-resolving inference variables\n    /// when reevaluating ambiguous goals. E.g. if we've got a goal `?x: Trait` with `?x`\n    /// already being constrained to `Vec<?y>`, then the first evaluation resolves it to\n    /// `Vec<?y>: Trait`. If this goal is still ambiguous and we later resolve `?y` to `u32`,\n    /// then reevaluating this goal now only needs to resolve `?y` while it would otherwise\n    /// have to resolve both `?x` and `?y`,\n    pub goal: Goal<I, I::Predicate>,\n    pub certainty: Certainty,\n    pub has_changed: HasChanged,\n    /// If the [`Certainty`] was `Maybe`, then keep track of whether the goal has changed\n    /// before rerunning it.\n    pub stalled_on: Option<GoalStalledOn<I>>,\n}\n\n/// The conditions that must change for a goal to warrant\n#[derive_where(Clone, Debug; I: Interner)]\npub struct GoalStalledOn<I: Interner> {\n    pub num_opaques: usize,\n    pub stalled_vars: Vec<I::GenericArg>,\n    pub sub_roots: Vec<TyVid>,\n    /// The certainty that will be returned on subsequent evaluations if this\n    /// goal remains stalled.\n    pub stalled_certainty: Certainty,\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":119,"oldLines":13,"newStart":119,"newLines":13,"lines":[" ","     #[instrument(level = \"trace\", skip(self))]","     fn compute_subtype_goal(&mut self, goal: Goal<I, ty::SubtypeRelation<I>>) -> QueryResult<I> {","-        match (goal.predicate.a.kind(), goal.predicate.b.kind()) {","+        match (goal.predicate.sub_ty.kind(), goal.predicate.super_ty.kind()) {","             (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {","                 self.sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(a_vid, b_vid);","                 self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)","             }","             _ => {","-                self.sub(goal.param_env, goal.predicate.a, goal.predicate.b)?;","+                self.sub(goal.param_env, goal.predicate.sub_ty, goal.predicate.super_ty)?;","                 self.evaluate_added_goals_and_make_canonical_response(Certainty::Yes)","             }","         }"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

std::ops::ControlFlow;\n\nuse rustc_data_structures::fx::FxHashSet;\nuse rustc_data_structures::graph::vec_graph::VecGraph;\nuse rustc_data_structures::graph::{self};\nuse rustc_data_structures::unord::{UnordMap, UnordSet};\nuse rustc_hir as hir;\nuse rustc_hir::HirId;\nuse rustc_hir::def::{DefKind, Res};\nuse rustc_hir::def_id::DefId;\nuse rustc_hir::intravisit::{InferKind, Visitor};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, Ty, TyCtxt, TypeSuperVisitable, TypeVisitable};\nuse rustc_session::lint;\nuse rustc_span::def_id::LocalDefId;\nuse rustc_span::{DUMMY_SP, Span};\nuse rustc_trait_selection::traits::{ObligationCause, ObligationCtxt};\nuse tracing::debug;\n\nuse crate::{FnCtxt, errors};\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone)]\npub(crate) enum DivergingFallbackBehavior {\n    /// Always fallback to `()` (aka \"always spontaneous decay\")\n    ToUnit,\n    /// Always fallback to `!` (which should be equivalent to never falling back + not making\n    /// never-to-any coercions unless necessary)\n    ToNever,\n    /// Don't fallback at all\n    NoFallback,\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> FnCtxt<'_, 'tcx> {\n    /// Performs type inference fallback, setting [`FnCtxt::diverging_fallback_has_occurred`]\n    /// if the never type fallback has occurred.\n    pub(super) fn type_inference_fallback(&self) {\n        debug!(\n            \"type-inference-fallback start obligations: {:#?}\",\n            self.fulfillment_cx.borrow_mut().pending_obligations()\n        );\n\n        // All type checking constraints were added, try to fallback unsolved variables.\n        self.select_obligations_where_possible(|_| {});\n\n        debug!(\n            \"type-inference-fallback post selection obligations: {:#?}\",\n            self.fulfillment_cx.borrow_mut().pending_obligations()\n        );\n\n        let fallback_occurred = self.fallback_types();\n\n        if fallback_occurred {\n            // if fallback occurred, previously stalled goals may make progress again\n            self.select_obligations_where_possible(|_| {});\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn fallback_types(&self) -> bool {\n        // Check if we have any unresolved variables. If not, no need for fallback.\n        let unresolved_variables = self.unresolved_variables();\n\n        if unresolved_variables.is_empty() {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let (diverging_fallback, diverging_fallback_ty) =\n            self.calculate_diverging_fallback(&unresolved_variables);\n\n        // We do fallback in two passes, to try to generate\n        // better error messages.\n        // The first time, we do *not* replace opaque types.\n        let mut fallback_occurred = false;\n        for ty in unresolved_variables {\n            debug!(\"unsolved_variable = {:?}\", ty);\n            fallback_occurred |=\n                self.fallback_if_possible(ty, &diverging_fallback, diverging_fallback_ty);\n        }\n\n        fallback_occurred\n    }\n\n    /// Tries to apply a fallback to `ty` if it is an unsolved variable.\n    ///\n    /// - Unconstrained ints are replaced with `i32`.\n    ///\n    /// - Unconstrained floats are replaced with `f64`.\n    ///\n    /// - Non-numerics may get replaced with `()` or `!`, depending on how they\n    ///   were categorized by [`Self::calculate_diverging_fallback`], crate's\n    ///   edition, and the setting of `#![rustc_never_type_options(fallback = ...)]`.\n    ///\n    /// Fallback becomes very dubious if we have encountered\n    /// type-checking errors. In that case, fallback to Error.\n    ///\n    /// Sets [`FnCtxt::diverging_fallback_has_occurred`] if never type fallback\n    /// is performed during this call.\n    fn fallback_if_possible(\n        &self,\n        ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n        diverging_fallback: &UnordSet<Ty<'tcx>>,\n        diverging_fallback_ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n    ) -> bool {\n        // Careful: we do NOT shallow-resolve `ty`. We know that `ty`\n        // is an unsolved variable, and we determine its fallback\n        // based solely on how it was created, not what other type\n        // variables it may have been unified with since then.\n        //\n        // The reason this matters is that other attempts at fallback\n        // may (in principle) conflict with this fallback, and we wish\n        // to generate a type error in that case. (However, this\n        // actually isn't true right now, because we're only using the\n        // builtin fallback rules. This would be true if we were using\n        // user-supplied fallbacks. But it's still useful to write the\n        // code to detect bugs.)\n        //\n        // (Note though that if we have a general type variable `?T`\n        // that is then unified with an integer type variable `?I`\n        // that ultimately never gets resolved to a special integral\n        // type, `?T` is not considered unsolved, but `?I` is. The\n        // same is true for float variables.)\n        let fallback = match ty.kind() {\n            _ if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() => Ty::new_error(self.tcx, e),\n            ty::Infer(ty::IntVar(_)) => self.tcx.types.i32,\n            ty::Infer(ty::FloatVar(_)) => self.tcx.types.f64,\n            _ if diverging_fallback.contains(&ty) => {\n                self.diverging_fallback_has_occurred.set(true);\n                diverging_fallback_ty\n            }\n            _ => return false,\n        };\n        debug!(\"fallback_if_possible(ty={:?}): defaulting to `{:?}`\", ty, fallback);\n\n        let span = ty.ty_vid().map_or(DUMMY_SP, |vid| self.infcx.type_var_origin(vid).span);\n        self.demand_eqtype(span, ty, fallback);\n        true\n    }\n\n    fn calculate_diverging_fallback(\n        &self,\n        unresolved_variables: &[Ty<'tcx>],\n    ) -> (UnordSet<Ty<'tcx>>, Ty<'tcx>) {\n        debug!(\"calculate_diverging_fallback({:?})\", unresolved_variables);\n\n        let diverging_fallback_ty = match self.diverging_fallback_behavior {\n            DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToUnit => self.tcx.types.unit,\n            DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToNever => self.tcx.types.never,\n            DivergingFallbackBehavior::NoFallback => {\n                // the type doesn't matter, since no fallback will occur\n                return (UnordSet::new(), self.tcx.types.unit);\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Construct a coercion graph where an edge `A -> B` indicates\n        // a type variable is that is coerced\n        let coercion_graph = self.create_coercion_graph();\n\n        // Extract the unsolved type inference variable vids; note that some\n        // unsolved variables are integer/float variables and are excluded.\n        let unsolved_vids = unresolved_variables.iter().filter_map(|ty| ty.ty_vid());\n\n        // Compute the diverging root vids D -- that is, the root vid of\n        // those type variables that (a) are the target of a coercion from\n        // a `!` type and (b) have not yet been solved.\n        //\n        // These variables are the ones that are targets for fallback to\n        // either `!` or `()`.\n        let diverging_roots: UnordSet<ty::TyVid> = self\n            .diverging_type_vars\n            .borrow()\n            .items()\n            .map(|&ty| self.shallow_resolve(ty))\n            .filter_map(|ty| ty.ty_vid())\n            .map(|vid| self.root_var(vid))\n            .collect();\n        debug!(\n            \"calculate_diverging_fallback: diverging_type_vars={:?}\",\n            self.diverging_type_vars.borrow()\n        );\n        debug!(\"calculate_diverging_fallback: diverging_roots={:?}\", diverging_roots);\n\n        // Find all type variables that are reachable from a diverging\n        // type variable. These will typically default to `!`, unless\n        // we find later that they are *also* reachable from some\n        // other type variable outside this set.\n        let mut diverging_vids = vec![];\n        for unsolved_vid in unsolved_vids {\n            let root_vid = self.root_var(unsolved_vid);\n            debug!(\n                \"calculate_diverging_fallback: unsolved_vid={:?} root_vid={:?} diverges={:?}\",\n                unsolved_vid,\n                root_vid,\n                diverging_roots.contains(&root_vid),\n            );\n            if diverging_roots.contains(&root_vid) {\n                diverging_vids.push(unsolved_vid);\n\n                debug!(\n                    \"calculate_diverging_fallback: root_vid={:?} reaches {:?}\",\n                    root_vid,\n                    graph::depth_first_search(&coercion_graph, root_vid).collect::<Vec<_>>()\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        debug!(\"obligations: {:#?}\", self.fulfillment_cx.borrow_mut().pending_obligations());\n\n        let mut diverging_fallback = UnordSet::with_capacity(diverging_vids.len());\n        let unsafe_infer_vars = OnceCell::new();\n\n        self.lint_obligations_broken_by_never_type_fallback_change(\n            &diverging_vids,\n            &coercion_graph,\n        );\n\n        for &diverging_vid in &diverging_vids {\n            let diverging_ty = Ty::new_var(self.tcx, diverging_vid);\n            let root_vid = self.root_var(diverging_vid);\n\n            self.lint_never_type_fallback_flowing_into_unsafe_code(\n                &unsafe_infer_vars,\n                &coercion_graph,\n                root_vid,\n            );\n\n            diverging_fallback.insert(diverging_ty);\n        }\n\n        (diverging_fallback, diverging_fallback_ty)\n    }\n\n    fn lint_never_type_fallback_flowing_into_unsafe_code(\n        &self,\n        unsafe_infer_vars: &OnceCell<UnordMap<ty::TyVid, (HirId, Span, UnsafeUseReason)>>,\n        coercion_graph: &VecGraph<ty::TyVid, true>,\n        root_vid: ty::TyVid,\n    ) {\n        let unsafe_infer_vars = unsafe_infer_vars.get_or_init(|| {\n            let unsafe_infer_vars = compute_unsafe_infer_vars(self, self.body_id);\n            debug!(?unsafe_infer_vars);\n            unsafe_infer_vars\n        });\n\n        let affected_unsafe_infer_vars =\n            graph::depth_first_search_as_undirected(&coercion_graph, root_vid)\n                .filter_map(|x| unsafe_infer_vars.get(&x).copied())\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n\n        let sugg = self.try_to_suggest_annotations(&[root_vid], coercion_graph);\n\n        for (hir_id, span, reason) in affected_unsafe_infer_vars {\n            self.tcx.emit_node_span_lint(\n                lint::builtin::NEVER_TYPE_FALLBACK_FLOWING_INTO_UNSAFE,\n                hir_id,\n                span,\n                match reason {\n                    UnsafeUseReason::Call => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::Call { sugg: sugg.clone() }\n                    }\n                    UnsafeUseReason::Method => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::Method { sugg: sugg.clone() }\n                    }\n                    UnsafeUseReason::Path => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::Path { sugg: sugg.clone() }\n                    }\n                    UnsafeUseReason::UnionField => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::UnionField {\n                            sugg: sugg.clone(),\n                        }\n                    }\n                    UnsafeUseReason::Deref => {\n                        errors::NeverTypeFallbackFlowingIntoUnsafe::Deref { sugg: sugg.clone() }\n                    }\n                },\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn lint_obligations_broken_by_never_type_fallback_change(\n        &self,\n        diverging_vids: &[ty::TyVid],\n        coercions: &VecGraph<ty::TyVid, true>,\n    ) {\n        let DivergingFallbackBehavior::ToUnit = self.diverging_fallback_behavior else { return };\n\n        // Fallback happens if and only if there are diverging variables\n        if diverging_vids.is_empty() {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Returns errors which happen if fallback is set to `fallback`\n        let remaining_errors_if_fallback_to = |fallback| {\n            self.probe(|_| {\n                let obligations = self.fulfillment_cx.borrow().pending_obligations();\n                let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new_with_diagnostics(&self.infcx);\n                ocx.register_obligations(obligations.iter().cloned());\n\n                for &diverging_vid in diverging_vids {\n                    let diverging_ty = Ty::new_var(self.tcx, diverging_vid);\n\n                    ocx.eq(&ObligationCause::dummy(), self.param_env, diverging_ty, fallback)\n                        .expect(\"expected diverging var to be unconstrained\");\n                }\n\n                ocx.try_evaluate_obligations()\n            })\n        };\n\n        // If we have no errors with `fallback = ()`, but *do* have errors with `fallback = !`,\n        // then this code will be broken by the never type fallback change.\n        let unit_errors = remaining_errors_if_fallback_to(self.tcx.types.unit);\n        if unit_errors.is_empty()\n            && let mut never_errors = remaining_errors_if_fallback_to(self.tcx.types.never)\n            && let [never_error, ..] = never_errors.as_mut_slice()\n        {\n            self.adjust_fulfillment_error_for_expr_obligation(never_error);\n            let sugg = self.try_to_suggest_annotations(diverging_vids, coercions);\n            self.tcx.emit_node_span_lint(\n                lint::builtin::DEPENDENCY_ON_UNIT_NEVER_TYPE_FALLBACK,\n                self.tcx.local_def_id_to_hir_id(self.body_id),\n                self.tcx.def_span(self.body_id),\n                errors::DependencyOnUnitNeverTypeFallback {\n                    obligation_span: never_error.obligation.cause.span,\n                    obligation: never_error.obligation.predicate,\n                    sugg,\n                },\n            )\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns a graph whose nodes are (unresolved) inference variables and where\n    /// an edge `?A -> ?B` indicates that the variable `?A` is coerced to `?B`.\n    fn create_coercion_graph(&self) -> VecGraph<ty::TyVid, true> {\n        let pending_obligations = self.fulfillment_cx.borrow_mut().pending_obligations();\n        debug!(\"create_coercion_graph: pending_obligations={:?}\", pending_obligations);\n        let coercion_edges: Vec<(ty::TyVid, ty::TyVid)> = pending_obligations\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter_map(|obligation| {\n                // The predicates we are looking for look like `Coerce(?A -> ?B)`.\n                // They will have no bound variables.\n                obligation.predicate.kind().no_bound_vars()\n            })\n            .filter_map(|atom| {\n                // We consider both subtyping and coercion to imply 'flow' from\n                // some position in the code `a` to a different position `b`.\n                // This is then used to determine which variables interact with\n                // live code, and as such must fall back to `()` to preserve\n                // soundness.\n                //\n                // In practice currently the two ways that this happens is\n                // coercion and subtyping.\n                let (a, b) = match atom {\n                    ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => (a, b),\n                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => {\n                        (a, b)\n                    }\n                    _ => return None,\n                };\n\n                let a_vid = self.root_vid(a)?;\n                let b_vid = self.root_vid(b)?;\n                Some((a_vid, b_vid))\n            })\n            .collect();\n        debug!(\"create_coercion_graph: coercion_edges={:?}\", coercion_edges);\n        let num_ty_vars = self.num_ty_vars();\n\n        VecGraph::new(num_ty_vars, coercion_edges)\n    }\n\n    /// If `ty` is an unresolved type variable, returns its root vid.\n    fn root_vid(&self, ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> Option<ty::TyVid> {\n        Some(self.root_var(self.shallow_resolve(ty).ty_vid()?))\n    }\n\n    /// Given a set of diverging vids and coercions, walk the HIR to gather a\n    /// set of suggestions which can be applied to preserve fallback to unit.\n    fn try_to_suggest_annotations(\n        &self,\n        diverging_vids: &[ty::TyVid],\n        coercions: &VecGraph<ty::TyVid, true>,\n    ) -> errors::SuggestAnnotations {\n        let body =\n            self.tcx.hir_maybe_body_owned_by(self.body_id).expect(\"body id must have an owner\");\n        // For each diverging var, look through the HIR for a place to give it\n        // a type annotation. We do this per var because we only really need one\n        // suggestion to influence a var to be `()`.\n        let suggestions = diverging_vids\n            .iter()\n            .copied()\n            .filter_map(|vid| {\n                let reachable_vids =\n                    graph::depth_first_search_as_undirected(coercions, vid).collect();\n                AnnotateUnitFallbackVisitor { reachable_vids, fcx: self }\n                    .visit_expr(body.value)\n                    .break_value()\n            })\n            .collect();\n        errors::SuggestAnnotations { suggestions }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Try to walk the HIR to find a place to insert a useful suggestion\n/// to preserve fallback to `()` in 2024.\nstruct AnnotateUnitFallbackVisitor<'a, 'tcx> {\n    reachable_vids: FxHashSet<ty::TyVid>,\n    fcx: &'a FnCtxt<'a, 'tcx>,\n}\nimpl<'tcx> AnnotateUnitFallbackVisitor<'_, 'tcx> {\n    // For a given path segment, if it's missing a turbofish, try to suggest adding\n    // one so we can constrain an argument to `()`. To keep the suggestion simple,\n    // we want to simply suggest `_` for all the other args. This (for now) only\n    // works when there are only type variables (and region variables, since we can\n    // elide them)...\n    fn suggest_for_segment(\n        &self,\n        arg_segment: &'tcx hir::PathSegment<'tcx>,\n        def_id: DefId,\n        id: HirId,\n    ) -> ControlFlow<errors::SuggestAnnotation> {\n        if arg_segment.args.is_none()\n            && let Some(all_args) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().node_args_opt(id)\n            && let generics = self.fcx.tcx.generics_of(def_id)\n            && let args = all_args[generics.parent_count..].iter().zip(&generics.own_params)\n            // We can't turbofish consts :(\n            && args.clone().all(|(_, param)| matches!(param.kind, ty::GenericParamDefKind::Type { .. } | ty::GenericParamDefKind::Lifetime))\n        {\n            // We filter out APITs, which are not turbofished.\n            let non_apit_type_args = args.filter(|(_, param)| {\n                matches!(param.kind, ty::GenericParamDefKind::Type { synthetic: false, .. })\n            });\n            let n_tys = non_apit_type_args.clone().count();\n            for (idx, (arg, _)) in non_apit_type_args.enumerate() {\n                if let Some(ty) = arg.as_type()\n                    && let Some(vid) = self.fcx.root_vid(ty)\n                    && self.reachable_vids.contains(&vid)\n                {\n                    return ControlFlow::Break(errors::SuggestAnnotation::Turbo(\n                        arg_segment.ident.span.shrink_to_hi(),\n                        n_tys,\n                        idx,\n                    ));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        ControlFlow::Continue(())\n    }\n}\nimpl<'tcx> Visitor<'tcx> for AnnotateUnitFallbackVisitor<'_, 'tcx> {\n    type Result = ControlFlow<errors::SuggestAnnotation>;\n\n    fn visit_infer(\n        &mut self,\n        inf_id: HirId,\n        inf_span: Span,\n        _kind: InferKind<'tcx>,\n    ) -> Self::Result {\n        // Try to replace `_` with `()`.\n        if let Some(ty) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().node_type_opt(inf_id)\n            && let Some(vid) = self.fcx.root_vid(ty)\n            && self.reachable_vids.contains(&vid)\n            && inf_span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            return ControlFlow::Break(errors::SuggestAnnotation::Unit(inf_span));\n        }\n\n        ControlFlow::Continue(())\n    }\n\n    fn visit_qpath(\n        &mut self,\n        qpath: &'tcx rustc_hir::QPath<'tcx>,\n        id: HirId,\n        span: Span,\n    ) -> Self::Result {\n        let arg_segment = match qpath {\n            hir::QPath::Resolved(_, path) => {\n                path.segments.last().expect(\"paths should have a segment\")\n            }\n            hir::QPath::TypeRelative(_, segment) => segment,\n        };\n        // Alternatively, try to turbofish `::<_, (), _>`.\n        if let Some(def_id) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().qpath_res(qpath, id).opt_def_id()\n            && span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            self.suggest_for_segment(arg_segment, def_id, id)?;\n        }\n        hir::intravisit::walk_qpath(self, qpath, id)\n    }\n\n    fn visit_expr(&mut self, expr: &'tcx hir::Expr<'tcx>) -> Self::Result {\n        if let hir::ExprKind::Closure(&hir::Closure { body, .. })\n        | hir::ExprKind::ConstBlock(hir::ConstBlock { body, .. }) = expr.kind\n        {\n            self.visit_body(self.fcx.tcx.hir_body(body))?;\n        }\n\n        // Try to suggest adding an explicit qself `()` to a trait method path.\n        // i.e. changing `Default::default()` to `<() as Default>::default()`.\n        if let hir::ExprKind::Path(hir::QPath::Resolved(None, path)) = expr.kind\n            && let Res::Def(DefKind::AssocFn, def_id) = path.res\n            && self.fcx.tcx.trait_of_assoc(def_id).is_some()\n            && let Some(args) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().node_args_opt(expr.hir_id)\n            && let self_ty = args.type_at(0)\n            && let Some(vid) = self.fcx.root_vid(self_ty)\n            && self.reachable_vids.contains(&vid)\n            && let [.., trait_segment, _method_segment] = path.segments\n            && expr.span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            let span = path.span.shrink_to_lo().to(trait_segment.ident.span);\n            return ControlFlow::Break(errors::SuggestAnnotation::Path(span));\n        }\n\n        // Or else, try suggesting turbofishing the method args.\n        if let hir::ExprKind::MethodCall(segment, ..) = expr.kind\n            && let Some(def_id) =\n                self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().type_dependent_def_id(expr.hir_id)\n            && expr.span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            self.suggest_for_segment(segment, def_id, expr.hir_id)?;\n        }\n\n        hir::intravisit::walk_expr(self, expr)\n    }\n\n    fn visit_local(&mut self, local: &'tcx hir::LetStmt<'tcx>) -> Self::Result {\n        // For a local, try suggest annotating the type if it's missing.\n        if let hir::LocalSource::Normal = local.source\n            && let None = local.ty\n            && let Some(ty) = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow().node_type_opt(local.hir_id)\n            && let Some(vid) = self.fcx.root_vid(ty)\n            && self.reachable_vids.contains(&vid)\n            && local.span.can_be_used_for_suggestions()\n        {\n            return ControlFlow::Break(errors::SuggestAnnotation::Local(\n                local.pat.span.shrink_to_hi(),\n            ));\n        }\n        hir::intravisit::walk_local(self, local)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone)]\npub(crate) enum UnsafeUseReason {\n    Call,\n    Method,\n    Path,\n    UnionField,\n    Deref,\n}\n\n/// Finds all type variables which are passed to an `unsafe` operation.\n///\n/// For example, for this function `f`:\n/// ```ignore (demonstrative)\n/// fn f() {\n///     unsafe {\n///         let x /* ?X */ = core::mem::zeroed();\n///         //               ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ -- hir_id, span, reason\n///\n///         let y = core::mem::zeroed::<Option<_ /* ?Y */>>();\n///         //      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ -- hir_id, span, reason\n///     }\n/// }\n/// ```\n///\n/// `compute_unsafe_infer_vars` will return `{ id(?X) -> (hir_id, span, Call) }`\nfn compute_unsafe_infer_vars<'a, 'tcx>(\n    fcx: &'a FnCtxt<'a, 'tcx>,\n    body_id: LocalDefId,\n) -> UnordMap<ty::TyVid, (HirId, Span, UnsafeUseReason)> {\n    let body = fcx.tcx.hir_maybe_body_owned_by(body_id).expect(\"body id must have an owner\");\n    let mut res = UnordMap::default();\n\n    struct UnsafeInferVarsVisitor<'a, 'tcx> {\n        fcx: &'a FnCtxt<'a, 'tcx>,\n        res: &'a mut UnordMap<ty::TyVid, (HirId, Span, UnsafeUseReason)>,\n    }\n\n    impl Visitor<'_> for UnsafeInferVarsVisitor<'_, '_> {\n        fn visit_expr(&mut self, ex: &'_ hir::Expr<'_>) {\n            let typeck_results = self.fcx.typeck_results.borrow();\n\n            match ex.kind {\n                hir::ExprKind::MethodCall(..) => {\n                    if let Some(def_id) = typeck_results.type_dependent_def_id(ex.hir_id)\n                        && let method_ty = self.fcx.tcx.type_of(def_id).instantiate_identity()\n                        && let sig = method_ty.fn_sig(self.fcx.tcx)\n                        && sig.safety().is_unsafe()\n                    {\n                        let mut collector = InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::Method),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        };\n\n                        // Collect generic arguments (incl. `Self`) of the method\n                        typeck_results\n                            .node_args(ex.hir_id)\n                            .types()\n                            .for_each(|t| t.visit_with(&mut collector));\n                    }\n                }\n\n                hir::ExprKind::Call(func, ..) => {\n                    let func_ty = typeck_results.expr_ty(func);\n\n                    if func_ty.is_fn()\n                        && let sig = func_ty.fn_sig(self.fcx.tcx)\n                        && sig.safety().is_unsafe()\n                    {\n                        let mut collector = InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::Call),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        };\n\n                        // Try collecting generic arguments of the function.\n                        // Note that we do this below for any paths (that don't have to be called),\n                        // but there we do it with a different span/reason.\n                        // This takes priority.\n                        typeck_results\n                            .node_args(func.hir_id)\n                            .types()\n                            .for_each(|t| t.visit_with(&mut collector));\n\n                        // Also check the return type, for cases like `returns_unsafe_fn_ptr()()`\n                        sig.output().visit_with(&mut collector);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Check paths which refer to functions.\n                // We do this, instead of only checking `Call` to make sure the lint can't be\n                // avoided by storing unsafe function in a variable.\n                hir::ExprKind::Path(_) => {\n                    let ty = typeck_results.expr_ty(ex);\n\n                    // If this path refers to an unsafe function, collect inference variables which may affect it.\n                    // `is_fn` excludes closures, but those can't be unsafe.\n                    if ty.is_fn()\n                        && let sig = ty.fn_sig(self.fcx.tcx)\n                        && sig.safety().is_unsafe()\n                    {\n                        let mut collector = InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::Path),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        };\n\n                        // Collect generic arguments of the function\n                        typeck_results\n                            .node_args(ex.hir_id)\n                            .types()\n                            .for_each(|t| t.visit_with(&mut collector));\n                    }\n                }\n\n                hir::ExprKind::Unary(hir::UnOp::Deref, pointer) => {\n                    if let ty::RawPtr(pointee, _) = typeck_results.expr_ty(pointer).kind() {\n                        pointee.visit_with(&mut InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::Deref),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        });\n                    }\n                }\n\n                hir::ExprKind::Field(base, _) => {\n                    let base_ty = typeck_results.expr_ty(base);\n\n                    if base_ty.is_union() {\n                        typeck_results.expr_ty(ex).visit_with(&mut InferVarCollector {\n                            value: (ex.hir_id, ex.span, UnsafeUseReason::UnionField),\n                            res: self.res,\n                        });\n                    }\n                }\n\n                _ => (),\n            };\n\n            hir::intravisit::walk_expr(self, ex);\n        }\n    }\n\n    struct InferVarCollector<'r, V> {\n        value: V,\n        res: &'r mut UnordMap<ty::TyVid, V>,\n    }\n\n    impl<'tcx, V: Copy> ty::TypeVisitor<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for InferVarCollector<'_, V> {\n        fn visit_ty(&mut self, t: Ty<'tcx>) {\n            if let Some(vid) = t.ty_vid() {\n                _ = self.res.try_insert(vid, self.value);\n            } else {\n                t.super_visit_with(self)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    UnsafeInferVarsVisitor { fcx, res: &mut res }.visit_expr(&body.value);\n\n    debug!(?res, \"collected the following unsafe vars for {body_id:?}\");\n\n    res\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":350,"oldLines":8,"newStart":350,"newLines":8,"lines":["                 // coercion and subtyping.","                 let (a, b) = match atom {","                     ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => (a, b),","-                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => compute_applicable_impls_for_diagnostics<'tcx>(\n    infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n    obligation: &PolyTraitObligation<'tcx>,\n) -> Vec<CandidateSource> {\n    let tcx = infcx.tcx;\n    let param_env = obligation.param_env;\n\n    let predicate_polarity = obligation.predicate.skip_binder().polarity;\n\n    let impl_may_apply = |impl_def_id| {\n        let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new(infcx);\n        infcx.enter_forall(obligation.predicate, |placeholder_obligation| {\n            let obligation_trait_ref = ocx.normalize(\n                &ObligationCause::dummy(),\n                param_env,\n                placeholder_obligation.trait_ref,\n            );\n\n            let impl_args = infcx.fresh_args_for_item(DUMMY_SP, impl_def_id);\n            let impl_trait_ref = tcx.impl_trait_ref(impl_def_id).instantiate(tcx, impl_args);\n            let impl_trait_ref =\n                ocx.normalize(&ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, impl_trait_ref);\n\n            if let Err(_) =\n                ocx.eq(&ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, obligation_trait_ref, impl_trait_ref)\n            {\n                return false;\n            }\n\n            let impl_trait_header = tcx.impl_trait_header(impl_def_id);\n            let impl_polarity = impl_trait_header.polarity;\n\n            match (impl_polarity, predicate_polarity) {\n                (ty::ImplPolarity::Positive, ty::PredicatePolarity::Positive)\n                | (ty::ImplPolarity::Negative, ty::PredicatePolarity::Negative) => {}\n                _ => return false,\n            }\n\n            let obligations = tcx\n                .predicates_of(impl_def_id)\n                .instantiate(tcx, impl_args)\n                .into_iter()\n                .map(|(predicate, _)| {\n                    Obligation::new(tcx, ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, predicate)\n                })\n                // Kinda hacky, but let's just throw away obligations that overflow.\n                // This may reduce the accuracy of this check (if the obligation guides\n                // inference or it actually resulted in error after others are processed)\n                // ... but this is diagnostics code.\n                .filter(|obligation| {\n                    infcx.next_trait_solver() || infcx.evaluate_obligation(obligation).is_ok()\n                });\n            ocx.register_obligations(obligations);\n\n            ocx.try_evaluate_obligations().is_empty()\n        })\n    };\n\n    let param_env_candidate_may_apply = |poly_trait_predicate: ty::PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>| {\n        let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new(infcx);\n        infcx.enter_forall(obligation.predicate, |placeholder_obligation| {\n            let obligation_trait_ref = ocx.normalize(\n                &ObligationCause::dummy(),\n                param_env,\n                placeholder_obligation.trait_ref,\n            );\n\n            let param_env_predicate = let param_env_trait_ref =\n                ocx.normalize(&ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, param_env_predicate.trait_ref);\n\n            if let Err(_) = ocx.eq(\n                &ObligationCause::dummy(),\n                param_env,\n                obligation_trait_ref,\n                param_env_trait_ref,\n            ) {\n                return false;\n            }\n\n            ocx.try_evaluate_obligations().is_empty()\n        })\n    };\n\n    let mut ambiguities = Vec::new();\n\n    tcx.for_each_relevant_impl(\n        obligation.predicate.def_id(),\n        obligation.predicate.skip_binder().trait_ref.self_ty(),\n        |impl_def_id| {\n            if infcx.probe(|_| impl_may_apply(impl_def_id)) {\n                ambiguities.push(CandidateSource::DefId(impl_def_id))\n            }\n        },\n    );\n\n    // If our `body_id` has been set (and isn't just from a dummy obligation cause),\n    // then try to look for a param-env clause that would apply. The way we compute\n    // this is somewhat manual, since we need the spans, so we elaborate this directly\n    // from `predicates_of` rather than actually looking at the param-env which\n    // otherwise would be more appropriate.\n    let body_id = obligation.cause.body_id;\n    if body_id != CRATE_DEF_ID {\n        let predicates = tcx.predicates_of(body_id.to_def_id()).instantiate_identity(tcx);\n        for (pred, span) in elaborate(tcx, predicates.into_iter()) {\n            let kind = pred.kind();\n            if let ty::ClauseKind::Trait(trait_pred) = kind.skip_binder()\n                && param_env_candidate_may_apply(kind.rebind(trait_pred))\n            {\n                if kind.rebind(trait_pred.trait_ref)\n                    == ty::Binder::dummy(ty::TraitRef::identity(tcx, trait_pred.def_id()))\n                {\n                    ambiguities.push(CandidateSource::ParamEnv(tcx.def_span(trait_pred.def_id())))\n                } else {\n                    ambiguities.push(CandidateSource::ParamEnv(span))\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    ambiguities\n}\n\nimpl<'a, 'tcx> TypeErrCtxt<'a, 'tcx> {\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"debug\")]\n    pub(super) fn maybe_report_ambiguity(\n        &self,\n        obligation: &PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n    ) -> ErrorGuaranteed {\n        // Unable to successfully determine, probably means\n        // insufficient type information, but could mean\n        // ambiguous impls. The latter *ought* to be a\n        // coherence violation, so we don't report it here.\n\n        let predicate = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(obligation.predicate);\n        let span = obligation.cause.span;\n        let mut long_ty_path = None;\n\n        debug!(?predicate, obligation.cause.code = ?obligation.cause.code());\n\n        // Ambiguity errors are often caused as fallout from earlier errors.\n        // We ignore them if this `infcx` is tainted in some cases below.\n\n        let bound_predicate = predicate.kind();\n        let mut err = match bound_predicate.skip_binder() {\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Trait(data)) => {\n                let trait_pred = bound_predicate.rebind(data);\n                debug!(?trait_pred);\n\n                if let Err(e) = predicate.error_reported() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n\n                if let Err(guar) = self.tcx.ensure_ok().coherent_trait(trait_pred.def_id()) {\n                    // Avoid bogus \"type annotations needed `Foo: Bar`\" errors on `impl Bar for Foo` in case\n                    // other `Foo` impls are incoherent.\n                    return guar;\n                }\n\n                // This is kind of a hack: it frequently happens that some earlier\n                // error prevents types from being fully inferred, and then we get\n                // a bunch of uninteresting errors saying something like \"<generic\n                // #0> doesn't implement Sized\". It may even be true that we\n                // could just skip over all checks where the self-ty is an\n                // inference variable, but I was afraid that there might be an\n                // inference variable created, registered as an obligation, and\n                // then never forced by writeback, and hence by skipping here we'd\n                // be ignoring the fact that we don't KNOW the type works\n                // out. Though even that would probably be harmless, given that\n                // we're only talking about builtin traits, which are known to be\n                // inhabited. We used to check for `self.tainted_by_errors()` to\n                // avoid inundating the user with unnecessary errors, but we now\n                // check upstream for type errors and don't add the obligations to\n                // begin with in those cases.\n                if matches!(\n                    self.tcx.as_lang_item(trait_pred.def_id()),\n                    Some(LangItem::Sized | LangItem::MetaSized)\n                ) {\n                    return match self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                        None => self\n                            .emit_inference_failure_err(\n                                obligation.cause.body_id,\n                                span,\n                                trait_pred.self_ty().skip_binder().into(),\n                                TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0282,\n                                false,\n                            )\n                            .emit(),\n                        Some(e) => e,\n                    };\n                }\n\n                // Typically, this ambiguity should only happen if\n                // there are unresolved type inference variables\n                // (otherwise it would suggest a coherence\n                // failure). But given #21974 that is not necessarily\n                // the case -- we can have multiple where clauses that\n                // are only distinguished by a region, which results\n                // in an ambiguity even when all types are fully\n                // known, since we don't dispatch based on region\n                // relationships.\n\n                // Pick the first generic parameter that still contains inference variables as the one\n                // we're going to emit an error for. If there are none (see above), fall back to\n                // a more general error.\n                let term = data\n                    .trait_ref\n                    .args\n                    .iter()\n                    .filter_map(ty::GenericArg::as_term)\n                    .find(|s| s.has_non_region_infer());\n\n                let mut err = if let Some(term) = term {\n                    self.emit_inference_failure_err(\n                        obligation.cause.body_id,\n                        span,\n                        term,\n                        TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0283,\n                        true,\n                    )\n                } else {\n                    struct_span_code_err!(\n                        self.dcx(),\n                        span,\n                        E0283,\n                        \"type annotations needed: cannot satisfy `{}`\",\n                        self.tcx.short_string(predicate, &mut long_ty_path),\n                    )\n                    .with_long_ty_path(long_ty_path)\n                };\n\n                let mut ambiguities = compute_applicable_impls_for_diagnostics(\n                    self.infcx,\n                    &obligation.with(self.tcx, trait_pred),\n                );\n                let has_non_region_infer = trait_pred\n                    .skip_binder()\n                    .trait_ref\n                    .args\n                    .types()\n                    .any(|t| !t.is_ty_or_numeric_infer());\n                // It doesn't make sense to talk about applicable impls if there are more than a\n                // handful of them. If there are a lot of them, but only a few of them have no type\n                // params, we only show those, as they are more likely to be useful/intended.\n                if ambiguities.len() > 5 {\n                    let infcx = self.infcx;\n                    if !ambiguities.iter().all(|option| match option {\n                        CandidateSource::DefId(did) => infcx.tcx.generics_of(*did).count() == 0,\n                        CandidateSource::ParamEnv(_) => true,\n                    }) {\n                        // If not all are blanket impls, we filter blanked impls out.\n                        ambiguities.retain(|option| match option {\n                            CandidateSource::DefId(did) => infcx.tcx.generics_of(*did).count() == 0,\n                            CandidateSource::ParamEnv(_) => true,\n                        });\n                    }\n                }\n                if ambiguities.len() > 1 && ambiguities.len() < 10 && has_non_region_infer {\n                    if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors()\n                        && term.is_none()\n                    {\n                        // If `arg.is_none()`, then this is probably two param-env\n                        // candidates or impl candidates that are equal modulo lifetimes.\n                        // Therefore, if we've already emitted an error, just skip this\n                        // one, since it's not particularly actionable.\n                        err.cancel();\n                        return e;\n                    }\n                    self.annotate_source_of_ambiguity(&mut err, &ambiguities, predicate);\n                } else {\n                    if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                        err.cancel();\n                        return e;\n                    }\n                    let pred = self.tcx.short_string(predicate, &mut err.long_ty_path());\n                    err.note(format!(\"cannot satisfy `{pred}`\"));\n                    let impl_candidates =\n                        self.find_similar_impl_candidates(predicate.as_trait_clause().unwrap());\n                    if impl_candidates.len() < 40 {\n                        self.report_similar_impl_candidates(\n                            impl_candidates.as_slice(),\n                            trait_pred,\n                            obligation.cause.body_id,\n                            &mut err,\n                            false,\n                            obligation.param_env,\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if let ObligationCauseCode::WhereClause(def_id, _)\n                | ObligationCauseCode::WhereClauseInExpr(def_id, ..) = *obligation.cause.code()\n                {\n                    self.suggest_fully_qualified_path(&mut err, def_id, span, trait_pred.def_id());\n                }\n\n                if term.is_some_and(|term| term.as_type().is_some())\n                    && let Some(body) = self.tcx.hir_maybe_body_owned_by(obligation.cause.body_id)\n                {\n                    let mut expr_finder = FindExprBySpan::new(span, self.tcx);\n                    expr_finder.visit_expr(&body.value);\n\n                    if let Some(hir::Expr {\n                        kind:\n                            hir::ExprKind::Call(\n                                hir::Expr {\n                                    kind: hir::ExprKind::Path(hir::QPath::Resolved(None, path)),\n                                    ..\n                                },\n                                _,\n                            )\n                            | hir::ExprKind::Path(hir::QPath::Resolved(None, path)),\n                        ..\n                    }) = expr_finder.result\n                        && let [\n                            ..,\n                            trait_path_segment @ hir::PathSegment {\n                                ident: assoc_item_ident,\n                                res: Res::Def(_, item_id),\n                                ..\n                            },\n                        ] = path.segments\n                        && data.trait_ref.def_id == *trait_id\n                        && self.tcx.trait_of_assoc(*item_id) == Some(*trait_id)\n                        && let None = self.tainted_by_errors()\n                    {\n                        let assoc_item = self.tcx.associated_item(item_id);\n                        let (verb, noun) = match assoc_item.kind {\n                            ty::AssocKind::Const { .. } => (\"refer to the\", \"constant\"),\n                            ty::AssocKind::Fn { .. } => (\"call\", \"function\"),\n                            // This is already covered by E0223, but this following single match\n                            // arm doesn't hurt here.\n                            ty::AssocKind::Type { .. } => (\"refer to the\", \"type\"),\n                        };\n\n                        // Replace the more general E0283 with a more specific error\n                        err.cancel();\n                        err = self.dcx().struct_span_err(\n                            span,\n                            format!(\n                                \"cannot {verb} associated {noun} on trait without specifying the \\\n                                 corresponding `impl` type\",\n                            ),\n                        );\n                        err.code(E0790);\n\n                        if item_id.is_local() {\n                            let trait_ident = self.tcx.item_name(*trait_id);\n                            err.span_label(\n                                self.tcx.def_span(*item_id),\n                                format!(\"`{trait_ident}::{assoc_item_ident}` defined here\"),\n                            );\n                        }\n\n                        err.span_label(span, format!(\"cannot {verb} associated {noun} of trait\"));\n\n                        let trait_impls =              {\n                            let non_blanket_impl_count =\n                                trait_impls.non_blanket_impls().values().flatten().count();\n                            // If there is only one implementation of the trait, suggest using it.\n                            // Otherwise, use a placeholder comment for the implementation.\n                            let (message, self_types) = if non_blanket_impl_count == 1 {\n                                (\n                                    \"use the fully-qualified path to the only available \\\n                                     implementation\",\n                                    vec![format!(\n                                        \"{}\",\n                                        self.tcx.type_of(impl_def_id).instantiate_identity()\n                                    )],\n                                )\n                            } else if non_blanket_impl_count < 20 {\n                                (\n                                    \"use a fully-qualified path to one of the available \\\n                                     implementations\",\n                                    trait_impls\n                                        .non_blanket_impls()\n                                        .values()\n                                        .flatten()\n                                        .map(|id| {\n                                            format!(\n                                                \"{}\",\n                                                self.tcx.type_of(id).instantiate_identity()\n                                            )\n                                        })\n                                        .collect::<Vec<String>>(),\n                                )\n                            } else {\n                                (\n                                    \"use a fully-qualified path                       };\n                            let suggestions: Vec<_> = self_types\n                                .into_iter()\n                                .map(|self_type| {\n                                    let mut suggestions = vec![(\n                                        path.span.shrink_to_lo(),\n                                        format!(\"<{self_type} as \"),\n                                    )];\n                                    if let Some(generic_arg) = trait_path_segment.args {\n                                        let between_span = trait_path_segment\n                                            .ident\n                                            .span\n                                            .between(generic_arg.span_ext);\n                                        // get rid of :: between Trait and <type>\n                                        // must be '::' between them, otherwise the parser won't accept the code\n                                        suggestions.push((between_span, \"\".to_string()));\n                                        suggestions.push((\n                                            generic_arg.span_ext.shrink_to_hi(),\n                                            \">\".to_string(),\n                                        ));\n                                    } else {\n                                        suggestions.push((\n                                            trait_path_segment.ident.span.shrink_to_hi(),\n                                            \">\".to_string(),\n                                        ));\n                                    }\n                                    suggestions\n                                })\n                                .collect();\n                            err.multipart_suggestions(\n                                message,\n                                suggestions,\n                                Applicability::MaybeIncorrect,\n                            );\n                        }\n                    }\n                };\n\n                err\n            }\n\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::WellFormed(term)) => {\n                // Same hacky approach as above to avoid deluging user\n                // with error messages.\n\n                if let Err(e) = term.error_reported() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n\n                self.emit_inference_failure_err(\n                    obligation.cause.body_id,\n                    span,\n                    term,\n                    TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0282,\n                    false,\n                )\n            }\n\n            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(data) => {\n                if let Err(e) = data.error_reported() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                let ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, a, b } = data;\n                // both must be type variables, or the other would've been instantiated\n                assert!(a.is_ty_var() && b.is_ty_var());\n                self.emit_inference_failure_err(\n                    obligation.cause.body_id,\n                    span,\n                    a.into(),\n                    TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0282,\n                    true,\n                )\n            }\n\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Projection(data)) => {\n                if let Err(e) = predicate.error_reported() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n\n                if let Err(guar) = self\n                    .tcx\n                    .ensure_ok()\n                    .coherent_trait(self.tcx.parent(data.projection_term.def_id))\n                {\n                    // Avoid bogus \"type annotations needed `Foo: Bar`\" errors on `impl Bar for Foo` in case\n                    // other `Foo` impls are incoherent.\n                    return guar;\n .filter_map(ty::GenericArg::as_term)\n                    .chain([data.term])\n                    .find(|g| g.has_non_region_infer());\n                let predicate = self.tcx.short_string(predicate, &mut long_ty_path);\n                if let Some(term) = term {\n                    self.emit_inference_failure_err(\n                        obligation.cause.body_id,\n                        span,\n                        term,\n                        TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0284,\n                        true,\n                    )\n                    .with_note(format!(\"cannot satisfy `{predicate}`\"))\n                    .with_long_ty_path(long_ty_path)\n                } else {\n                    // If we can't find a generic parameter, just print a generic error\n                    struct_span_code_err!(\n                        self.dcx(),\n                        span,\n                        E0284,\n .with_long_ty_path(long_ty_path)\n                }\n            }\n\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(data)) => {\n                if let Err(e) = predicate.error_reported() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n        post,\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nstruct HasNumericInferVisitor;\n\nimpl<'tcx> ty::TypeVisitor<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for HasNumericInferVisitor {\n    type Result = /// that are local to a particular fn/inferctxt\n        const HAS_FREE_LOCAL_REGIONS      = 1 << 9;\n\n        /// `true` if there are \"names\" of types and regions and so forth\n        /// that                        | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INFER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_INFER.bits()\n                                          | `Ty::outer_exclusive_binder` for details\n    pub outer_exclusive_binder: ty::DebruijnIndex,\n\n    interner: std::marker::PhantomData<I>,\n}\n\nimpl<I: Interner> FlagComputation<I> {\n    fn new() -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        FlagComputation {\n            flags: TypeFlags::empty(),\n            outer_exclusive_binder: ty::INNERMOST,\n            interner: std::marker::PhantomData,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[allow(rustc::usage_of_ty_tykind)]\n    pub fn for_kind(kind: &ty::TyKind<I>) -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        let mut result = FlagComputation::new();\n        result.add_kind(kind);\n        result\n    }\n\n    pub fn for_predicate(binder: ty::Binder<I, ty::PredicateKind<I>>) -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        let mut result = FlagComputation::new();\n        result.add_predicate(binder);\n        result\n    }\n\n    pub fn for_const_kind(kind: &ty::ConstKind<I>) -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        let mut result = FlagComputation::new();\n        result.add_const_kind(kind);\n        result\n    }\n\n    pub fn for_clauses(clauses: &[I::Clause]) -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        let mut result = FlagComputation::new();\n        for c in clauses {\n            result.add_flags(c.as_predicate().flags());\n            result.add_exclusive_binder(c.as_predicate().outer_exclusive_binder());\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    fn add_flags(&mut self, flags: TypeFlags) {\n        self.flags = self.flags | flags;\n    }\n\n    /// indicates that `self` refers to something at binding level `binder`\n    fn add_bound_var(&mut self, binder: ty::DebruijnIndex) {\n        let exclusive_binder = binder.shifted_in(1);\n        self.add_exclusive_binder(exclusive_binder);\n    }\n\n    /// indicates that `self` refers to something *inside* binding\n    /// level `binder` -- not bound by `binder`, but bound by the next\n    /// binder internal to it\n    fn add_exclusive_binder(&mut self, exclusive_binder: ty::DebruijnIndex) {\n        self.outer_exclusive_binder = self.outer_exclusive_binder.max(exclusive_binder);\n    }\n\n    /// Adds the flags/depth from a set of types that appear within the current type, but within a\n    /// region binder.\n    fn bound_computation<T, F>(&mut self, value: ty::Binder<I, T>, f: F)\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&mut Self, T),\n    {\n        let mut computation = FlagComputation::new();\n\n        if !value.bound_vars().is_empty() {\n            computation.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_BINDER_VARS);\n        }\n\n        f(&mut computation, value.skip_binder());\n\n        self.add_flags(computation.flags);\n\n        // The types that contributed to `computation` occurred within\n        // a region binder, so subtract one from the region depth\n        // within when adding the depth to `self`.\n        let outer_exclusive_binder = computation.outer_exclusive_binder;\n        if outer_exclusive_binder > ty::INNERMOST {\n            self.add_exclusive_binder(outer_exclusive_binder.shifted_out(1));\n        } // otherwise, this binder captures nothing\n    }\n\n    #[allow(rustc::usage_of_ty_tykind)]\n    fn add_kind(&mut self, kind: &ty::TyKind<I>) {\n        match *kind {\n            ty::Bool\n            | ty::Char\n            | ty::Int(_)\n            | ty::Float(_)\n            | ty::Uint(_)\n            | ty::Never\n            | ty::Str\n            | ty::Foreign(..) => {}\n\n            ty::Error(_) => self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_ERROR),\n\n            ty::Param(_) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PARAM);\n            }\n\n            ty::Closure(_, args)\n            | ty::CoroutineClosure(_, args)\n            | ty::CoroutineWitness(_, args) => {\n                self.add_args(args.as_slice());\n            }\n\n            ty::Coroutine(_, args) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_CORO);\n                self.add_args(args.as_slice());\n            }\n\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Canonical, _) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_BOUND);\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CANONICAL_BOUND);\n            }\n\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn), _) => {\n                self.add_bound_var(debruijn);\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_BOUND);\n            }\n\n            ty::Placeholder(..) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PLACEHOLDER);\n            }\n\n            ty::Infer(infer) => match infer {\n                ty::FreshTy(_) | ty::FreshIntTy(_) | ty::FreshFloatTy(_) => {\n                    self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_FRESH)\n                }\n\n                ty::TyVar(_) | ty::IntVar(_) | ty::FloatVar(_) => {\n                    self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INFER)\n                }\n            },\n\n            ty::Adt(_, args) => {\n                self.add_args(args.as_slice());\n            }\n\n            ty::Alias(kind, data) => {\n                self.add_flags(match kind {\n                    ty::Projection => TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PROJECTION,\n                    ty::Free => TypeFlags::HAS_TY_FREE_ALIAS,\n                    ty::Opaque => TypeFlags::HAS_TY_OPAQUE,\n                    ty::Inherent => TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INHERENT,\n                });\n\n                self.add_alias_ty(data);\n            }\n\n            ty::Dynamic(obj, r) => {\n                for predicate in obj.iter() {\n                    self.bound_computation(predicate, |computation, predicate| match predicate {\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::Trait(tr) => {\n                            computation.add_args(tr.args.as_slice())\n                        }\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::Projection(p) => {\n                            computation.add_existential_projection(&p);\n                        }\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(_) => {}\n                    });\n                }\n\n                self.add_region(r);\n            }\n\n            ty::Array(tt, len) => {\n                self.add_ty(tt);\n                self.add_const(len);\n            }\n\n            ty::Pat(ty, pat) => {\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n                self.add_ty_pat(pat);\n            }\n\n            ty::Slice(tt) => self.add_ty(tt),\n\n            ty::RawPtr(ty, _) => {\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n            }\n\n            ty::Ref(r, ty, _) => {\n                self.add_region(r);\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n            }\n\n            ty::Tuple(types) => {\n                self.add_tys(types);\n            }\n\n            ty::FnDef(_, args) => {\n                self.add_args(args.as_slice());\n            }\n\n            ty::FnPtr(sig_tys, _) => self.bound_computation(sig_tys, |computation, sig_tys| {\n                computation.add_tys(sig_tys.inputs_and_output);\n            }),\n\n            ty::UnsafeBinder(bound_ty) => {\n                self.bound_computation(bound_ty.into(), |computation, ty| {\n                    computation.add_ty(ty);\n                })\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_ty_pat(&mut self, pat: <I as Interner>::Pat) {\n        self.add_flags(pat.flags());\n        self.add_exclusive_binder(pat.outer_exclusive_binder());\n    }\n\n    fn add_predicate(&mut self, binder: ty::Binder<I, ty::PredicateKind<I>>) {\n        self.bound_computation(binder, |computation, atom| computation.add_predicate_atom(atom));\n    }\n\n    fn add_predicate_atom(&mut self, atom: ty::PredicateKind<I>) {\n        match atom {\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Trait(trait_pred)) => {\n                self.add_args(trait_pred.trait_ref.args.as_slice());\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect(ty::HostEffectPredicate {\n                trait_ref,\n                constness: _,\n            })) => {\n                self.add_args(trait_ref.args.as_slice());\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(ty::OutlivesPredicate(\n                a,\n                b,\n            ))) => {\n                self.add_region(a);\n                self.add_region(b);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(ty::OutlivesPredicate(\n                ty,\n                region,\n            ))) => {\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n                self.add_region(region);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(ct, ty)) => {\n                self.add_const(ct);\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => {\n                self.add_ty(a);\n                self.add_ty(b);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {\n                self.add_ty(a);\n                self.add_ty(b);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Projection(ty::ProjectionPredicate {\n                projection_term,\n                term,\n            })) => {\n self.add_term(term);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::DynCompatible(_def_id) => {}\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(uv)) => {\n ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(t1, t2, _) => {\n                self.add_term(t1);\n                self.add_term(t2);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(_sym)) => {}\n            ty::PredicateKind::Ambiguous => {}\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_ty(&mut self, ty: I::Ty) {\n        self.add_flags(ty.flags());\n        self.add_exclusive_binder(ty.outer_exclusive_binder());\n    }\n\n    fn add_tys(&mut self, tys: I::Tys) {\n        for ty in tys.iter() {\n            self.add_ty(ty);\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_region(&mut self, r: I::Region) {\n        self.add_flags(r.flags());\n        if let ty::ReBound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn), _) = r.kind()
