self.add_exclusive_binder(c.outer_exclusive_binder());\n    }\n\n    fn add_const_kind(&mut self, c: &ty::ConstKind<I>) {\n        match *c {\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n ty::ConstKind::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn), _) => {\n                self.add_bound_var(debruijn);\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_BOUND);\n            }\n }\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PARAM);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PLACEHOLDER);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Value(cv) => self.add_ty(cv.ty()),\n            ty::ConstKind::Expr(e) => self.add_args(e.args().as_slice()),\n            ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_ERROR),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_existential_projection(&mut self, projection: &ty::ExistentialProjection<I>) {\n        self.add_args(projection.args.as_slice());\n        match projection.term.kind() {\n            ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => self.add_ty(ty),\n            ty::TermKind::Const(ct) => self.add_const(ct),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_alias_ty(&mut self, alias_ty: ty::AliasTy<I>) {\n        self.add_args(alias_ty.args.as_slice());\n    }\n\n    fn {\n            match arg.kind() {\n                ty::GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => self.add_ty(ty),\n                ty::GenericArgKind::Lifetime(lt) => self.add_region(lt),\n                ty::GenericArgKind::Const(ct) => self.add_const(ct),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_term(&mut self, term: I::Term) {\n        match term.kind() {\n            ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => self.shallow_resolve(trait_pred.self_ty().skip_binder()).is_ty_var()\n                // We don't do this fast path when opaques are defined since we may\n                // eventually use opaques to self.inner.borrow_mut().opaque_types().is_empty()\n            {\n                return Some(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS);\n            }\n\n            if trait_pred.polarity() == `Copy`/`Clone` may be shadowed by a param-env candidate which\n                        // could force a lifetime error or guide inference. While that's\n                        // not generally desirable, it is observable, so for now let's\n                        // ignore this fast path for types that have regions or infer.\n SubregionOrigin::RelateRegionParamBound(span, None),\n                    outlives.1,\n                    outlives.0,\n                );\n                Some(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n ty::Predicate<'tcx>>,\n        span: Span,\n    ) -> Option<Certainty> {\n        if let Some(trait_pred) = goal.predicate.as_trait_clause() {\n            if self.shallow_resolve(trait_pred.self_ty().skip_binder()).is_ty_var()\n                // We don't do this fast path when opaques are defined since we may\n                // eventually use opaques to incompletely guide inference via ty var\n                // self types.\n                // FIXME: Properly consider opaques here.\n                && self.inner.borrow_mut().opaque_types().is_empty()\n            {\n                return Some(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS);\n            }\n\n            if trait_pred.polarity() == ty::PredicatePolarity::Positive {\n                match self.0.tcx.as_lang_item(trait_pred.def_id()) {\n                    Some(LangItem::Sized) | Some(LangItem::MetaSized) => {\n                        let predicate = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(goal.predicate);\n                        if sizedness_fast_path(self.tcx, predicate, goal.param_env) {\n                            return Some(Certainty::Yes);\n                        }\n                    }\n                    Some(LangItem::Copy | LangItem::Clone) => {\n                        let self_ty =\n                            self.resolve_vars_if_possible(trait_pred.self_ty().skip_binder());\n                        // Unlike `Sized` traits, which always prefer the built-in impl,\n                        // `Copy`/`Clone` may be shadowed by a param-env candidate which\n                        // could force a lifetime error or guide inference. While that's\n                        // not generally desirable, it is observable, so for now let's\n                        // ignore this fast path for types that have regions or infer.\n SubregionOrigin::RelateRegionParamBound(span, None),\n                    outlives.1,\n                    outlives.0,\n                );\n                Some(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n self.next_region_var(RegionVariableOrigin::Misc(span)).into()\n            }\n            ty::GenericArgKind::Type(_) => self.next_ty_var(span).into(),\n            ty::GenericArgKind::Const(_) => DeeplyNormalize(ty::AliasTerm<'tcx>),\n    TraitSolver(ty::Predicate<'tcx>),\n}\n\npub fn suggest_new_overflow_limit<'tcx, G: EmissionGuarantee>(\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n    err: &mut Diag<'_, G>,\n) {\n    let suggested_limit = match tcx.recursion_limit() {\n        Limit(0) => Limit(2),\n        limit => limit * 2,\n    };\n    err.help(format!(\n        \"consider increasing the recursion limit We\n    /// halt compilation unconditionally because it is important that\n    /// overflows never be masked -- they basically represent computations\n    /// whose result could not be truly determined and thus we can't say\n    /// if the program type checks or not -- and they are unusual\n    /// occurrences in any case.\n    pub fn report_overflow_error(\n        &self,\n        cause: span, suggest_increasing_limit);\n        mutate(&mut err);\n        err.emit().raise_fatal();\n    }\n\n    pub fn build_overflow_error(\n        &self,\n        cause: OverflowCause<'tcx>,\n        span: Span,\n        suggest_increasing_limit: bool,\n    ) -> Diag<'a> {\n        fn with_short_path<'tcx, T>(tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, value: T) -> String\n        where\n            T: fmt::Display + Print<'tcx, FmtPrinter<'tcx, 'tcx>>,\n        {\n            let s = value.to_string();\n            if s.len() > 50 {\n                // We don't need to save the type to a file, we will be talking about this type already\n                // in a separate note when we explain the obligation, so it will be available that way.\n                let mut p: FmtPrinter<'_, '_> =\n                    FmtPrinter::new_with_limit(tcx, Namespace::TypeNS, Limit(6));\n                value.print(&mut p).unwrap();\n                p.into_buffer()\n            } else {\n                s\n            }\n             span,\n                    E0275,\n                    \"overflow normalizing the {kind} `{alias_str}`\",\n                )\n            }\n This is equivalent to `report_overflow_obligation` except\n    /// that we can give a more helpful error message (and, in particular,\n    /// we do not suggest increasing the overflow limit, which is not\n    /// going to help).\n    pub fn report_overflow_obligation_cycle(&self, cycle: &[PredicateObligation<'tcx>]) -> ! {\n        let cycle = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(cycle.to_owned());\n        assert!(!cycle.is_empty());\n\n        debug!(?cycle, \"report_overflow_error_cycle\");\n\n        // The 'deepest' obligation is most likely to have a useful\n        // cause 'backtrace'\n        self.report_overflow_obligation(\n            cycle.iter().max_by_key(|p| p.recursion_depth).unwrap(),\n            false,\n        );\n    }\n\n    pub fn report_overflow_no_abort(\n        &self,\n rustc_middle::traits::solve::Goal;\nuse rustc_middle::ty::relate::combine::{super_combine_consts, super_combine_tys};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::relate::{\n    Relate, RelateResult, TypeRelation, relate_args_invariantly, relate_args_with_variances,\n};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, DelayedSet, Ty, TyCtxt, TyVar};\nuse rustc_span::Span;\nuse tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse crate::infer::BoundRegionConversionTime::HigherRankedType;\nuse crate::infer::relate::{PredicateEmittingRelation, StructurallyRelateAliases};\nuse crate::infer::{DefineOpaqueTypes, InferCtxt, SubregionOrigin, TypeTrace};\nuse crate::traits::{Obligation, PredicateObligations};\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub(crate) struct TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n\n    // Immutable fields\n    trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    obligations: PredicateObligations<'tcx>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `InferCtxt` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// obligations to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added\n    /// once or multiple times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, Ty<'tcx>, Ty<'tcx>)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, 'tcx> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    pub(crate) fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n        trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    ) -> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n        assert!(!infcx.next_trait_solver);\n        TypeRelating {\n            infcx,\n            trace,\n            param_env,\n            define_opaque_types,\n            ambient_variance,\n            obligations: PredicateObligations::new(),\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn into_obligations(self) -> PredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n        self.obligations\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeRelation<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n        self.infcx.tcx\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: rustc_hir::def_id::DefId,\n        a_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n        b_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: ty::VarianceDiagInfo<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) -> RelateResult<'tcx, Ty<'tcx>> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_id)), &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                a,\n                                b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().equate(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            (\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: a_def_id, .. }),\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: b_def_id, .. }),\n            ) if a_def_id == b_def_id => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }), _)\n            | (_, &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }))\n                if self.define_opaque_types == DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes && def_id.is_local() =>\n            {\n                self.register_goals(infcx.handle_opaque_type(\n                    a,\n                    b,\n                    self.trace.cause.span,\n                    self.param_env(),\n                )?);\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Region<'tcx>> {\n        let origin = SubregionOrigin::Subtype(Box::new(self.trace.clone()));\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, b, a);\n            }\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_eqregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Const<'tcx>> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Binder<'tcx, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if a == b {\n            // Do nothing\n        } else if let Some(a) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a, b)?;\n        } else {\n            let span = self.trace.cause.span;\n            let infcx = self.infcx;\n\n            match self.ambient_variance {\n                // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n                //\n                // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n                // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n                // the subtype.\n                //\n                // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n                // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n                // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n                // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n                //\n                // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n                // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n                //\n                // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n                ty::Covariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Contravariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n\n                // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n                // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n                //\n                // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n                // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n                // equating the instantiated types.\n                //\n                // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n                // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n                ty::Invariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n\n                    // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Bivariant => {\n                    unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> PredicateEmittingRelation<InferCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn span(&self) -> Span {\n        self.trace.span()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> ty::ParamEnv<'tcx> {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        StructurallyRelateAliases::No\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        preds: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>,\n    ) {\n        self.obligations.extend(preds.into_iter().map(|pred| {\n            Obligation::new(self.infcx.tcx, self.trace.cause.clone(), self.param_env, pred)\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, goals: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>) {\n        self.obligations.extend(goals.into_iter().map(|goal| {\n            Obligation::new(\n                self.infcx.tcx,\n                self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                goal.param_env,\n                goal.predicate,\n            )\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":140,"oldLines":8,"newStart":140,"newLines":8,"lines":["                             self.param_env,"," relate_args_invariantly, relate_args_with_variances,\n};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, DelayedSet, Ty, TyCtxt, TyVar};\nuse rustc_span::Span;\nuse tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse crate::infer::BoundRegionConversionTime::HigherRankedType;\nuse crate::infer::relate::{PredicateEmittingRelation, StructurallyRelateAliases};\nuse crate::infer::{DefineOpaqueTypes, InferCtxt, SubregionOrigin, TypeTrace};\nuse crate::traits::{Obligation, PredicateObligations};\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub(crate) struct TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n\n    // Immutable fields\n    trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    obligations: PredicateObligations<'tcx>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `InferCtxt` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// obligations to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added\n    /// once or multiple times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, Ty<'tcx>, Ty<'tcx>)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, 'tcx> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    pub(crate) fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n        trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    ) -> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n        assert!(!infcx.next_trait_solver);\n        TypeRelating {\n            infcx,\n            trace,\n            param_env,\n            define_opaque_types,\n            ambient_variance,\n            obligations: PredicateObligations::new(),\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn into_obligations(self) -> PredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n        self.obligations\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeRelation<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n        self.infcx.tcx\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: rustc_hir::def_id::DefId,\n        a_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n        b_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: ty::VarianceDiagInfo<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) -> RelateResult<'tcx, Ty<'tcx>> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_id)), &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                sub_ty: a,\n                                super_ty: b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().equate(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            (\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: a_def_id, .. }),\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: b_def_id, .. }),\n            ) if a_def_id == b_def_id => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }), _)\n            | (_, &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }))\n                if self.define_opaque_types == DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes && def_id.is_local() =>\n            {\n                self.register_goals(infcx.handle_opaque_type(\n                    a,\n                    b,\n                    self.trace.cause.span,\n                    self.param_env(),\n                )?);\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Region<'tcx>> {\n        let origin = SubregionOrigin::Subtype(Box::new(self.trace.clone()));\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, b, a);\n            }\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_eqregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Const<'tcx>> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Binder<'tcx, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if a == b {\n            // Do nothing\n        } else if let Some(a) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a, b)?;\n        } else {\n            let span = self.trace.cause.span;\n            let infcx = self.infcx;\n\n            match self.ambient_variance {\n                // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n                //\n                // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n                // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n                // the subtype.\n                //\n                // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n                // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n                // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n                // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n                //\n                // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n                // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n                //\n                // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n                ty::Covariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Contravariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n\n                // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n                // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n                //\n                // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n                // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n                // equating the instantiated types.\n                //\n                // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n                // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n                ty::Invariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n\n                    // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Bivariant => {\n                    unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> PredicateEmittingRelation<InferCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn span(&self) -> Span {\n        self.trace.span()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> ty::ParamEnv<'tcx> {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        StructurallyRelateAliases::No\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        preds: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>,\n    ) {\n        self.obligations.extend(preds.into_iter().map(|pred| {\n            Obligation::new(self.infcx.tcx, self.trace.cause.clone(), self.param_env, pred)\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, goals: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>) {\n        self.obligations.extend(goals.into_iter().map(|goal| {\n            Obligation::new(\n                self.infcx.tcx,\n                self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                goal.param_env,\n                goal.predicate,\n            )\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant fields.iter().map(|ty| ty.stable(tables, cx)).collect(),\n            )),\n            ty::Alias(alias_kind, alias_ty) => {\n                TyKind::Alias(alias_kind.stable(tables, cx), alias_ty.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::Param(param_ty) => TyKind::Param(param_ty.stable(tables, cx)),\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Canonical, _) => {\n                unreachable!()\n            }\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn_idx), bound_ty) => {\n                TyKind::Bound(debruijn_idx.as_usize(), bound_ty.stable(tables, cx))\n            }\n            ty::CoroutineWitness(def_id, args) => TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::CoroutineWitness(\n                tables.coroutine_witness_def(*def_id),\n                args.stable(tables, cx),\n            )),\n            ty::Placeholder(..) | ty::Infer(_) | ty::Error(_) => {\n                unreachable!();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::Pattern<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::Pattern;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        match **self {\n            ty::PatternKind::Range { start, end } => crate::ty::Pattern::Range {\n                // FIXME(SMIR): update data structures to not have an Option here anymore\n                start: Some(start.stable(tables, cx)),\n                end: Some(end.stable(tables, cx)),\n                include_end: true,\n            },\n            ty::PatternKind::NotNull => todo!(),\n            ty::PatternKind::Or(_) => todo!(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::Const<'tcx> {\n    type T = crate::ty::TyConst;\n\n    fn stable<'cx>(\n        &self,\n        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        let ct = cx.lift(*self).unwrap();\n        let kind = match ct.kind() {\n            ty::ConstKind::Value(cv) => {\n                let const_val = cx.valtree_to_const_val(cv);\n                if matches!(const_val, mir::ConstValue::ZeroSized) {\n                    crate::ty::TyConstKind::ZSTValue(cv.ty.stable(tables, cx))\n                } else {\n                    crate::ty::TyConstKind::Value(\n                        cv.ty.stable(tables, cx),\n                        alloc::new_allocation(cv.ty, const_val, tables, cx),\n                    )\n                }\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(param) => crate::ty::TyConstKind::Param(param.stable(tables, cx)),\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => crate::ty::TyConstKind::Unevaluated(\n                tables.const_def(uv.def),\n                uv.args.stable(tables, cx),\n            ),\n            ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => unreachable!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) => unreachable!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) => unimplemented!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => unimplemented!(),\n        };\n        let id = tables.intern_ty_const(ct);\n        crate::ty::TyConst::new(kind, id)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::ParamConst {\n    type T = crate::ty::ParamConst;\n    fn stable(&self, _: &mut Tables<'_, BridgeTys>, _: &CompilerCtxt<'_, BridgeTys>) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::ParamConst;\n        ParamConst { index: self.index, name: self.name.to_string() }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,\n    ) -> Self::T {\n        use crate::ty::Generics;\n\n        let params: Vec<_> = self.own_params.iter().map(|param| param.stable(tables, cx)).collect();\n        let param_def_id_to_index =\n            params.iter().map(|param| (param.def_id, param.index)).collect();\n\n        Generics {\n            parent: self.parent.map(|did| tables.generic_def(did)),\n            parent_count: self.parent_count,\n            params,\n            param_def_id_to_index,\n            has_self: self.has_self,\n            has_late_bound_regions: self\n                .has_late_bound_regions\n                .as_ref()\n                .map(|late_bound_regions| late_bound_regions.stable(tables, cx)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for
