supertrait_def_ids, supertraits, transitive_bounds_that_define_assoc_item,\n    upcast_choices, with_replaced_escaping_bound_vars,\n};\nuse crate::error_reporting::InferCtxtErrorExt;\nuse crate::infer::outlives::env::OutlivesEnvironment;\nuse crate::infer::{InferCtxt, TyCtxtInferExt};\nuse crate::regions::InferCtxtRegionExt;\nuse crate::traits::query::evaluate_obligation::InferCtxtExt as _;\n\n#[derive(Debug, TypeVisitable)]\npub struct FulfillmentError<'tcx> {\n    pub obligation: PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n    pub code: FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx>,\n    /// Diagnostics only: the 'root' obligation which resulted in\n    /// the failure to process `obligation`. This is the obligation\n    /// that was initially passed to `register_predicate_obligation`\n    pub root_obligation: PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> FulfillmentError<'tcx> {\n    pub fn new(\n        obligation: PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n        code: FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx>,\n        root_obligation: PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n    ) -> FulfillmentError<'tcx> {\n        FulfillmentError { obligation, code, root_obligation }\n    }\n\n    pub fn is_true_error(&self) -> bool {\n        match self.code {\n            FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(_)\n            | FulfillmentErrorCode::Project(_)\n            | FulfillmentErrorCode::Subtype(_, _)\n            | FulfillmentErrorCode::ConstEquate(_, _) => true,\n            FulfillmentErrorCode::Cycle(_) | FulfillmentErrorCode::Ambiguity { overflow: _ } => {\n                false\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, TypeVisitable)]\npub enum FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx> {\n    /// Inherently impossible to fulfill; this trait is implemented if and only\n    /// if it is already implemented.\n    Cycle(PredicateObligations<'tcx>),\n    Select(SelectionError<'tcx>),\n    Project(MismatchedProjectionTypes<'tcx>),\n    Subtype(ExpectedFound<Ty<'tcx>>, TypeError<'tcx>), // always comes from a SubtypePredicate\n    ConstEquate(ExpectedFound<ty::Const<'tcx>>, TypeError<'tcx>),\n    Ambiguity {\n        /// Overflow is only `Some(suggest_recursion_limit)` when using the next generation\n        /// trait solver `-Znext-solver`. With the old solver overflow is eagerly handled by\n        /// emitting a fatal error instead.\n        overflow: Option<bool>,\n    },\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Debug for FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx> {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match *self {\n            FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(ref e) => write!(f, \"{e:?}\"),\n            FulfillmentErrorCode::Project(ref e) => write!(f, \"{e:?}\"),\n            FulfillmentErrorCode::Subtype(ref a, ref b) => {\n                write!(f, \"CodeSubtypeError({a:?}, {b:?})\")\n            }\n            FulfillmentErrorCode::ConstEquate(ref a, ref b) => {\n                write!(f, \"CodeConstEquateError({a:?}, {b:?})\")\n            }\n            FulfillmentErrorCode::Ambiguity { overflow: None } => write!(f, \"Ambiguity\"),\n            FulfillmentErrorCode::Ambiguity { overflow: Some(suggest_increasing_limit) } => {\n                write!(f, \"Overflow({suggest_increasing_limit})\")\n            }\n            FulfillmentErrorCode::Cycle(ref cycle) => write!(f, \"Cycle({cycle:?})\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Whether to skip the leak check, as part of a future compatibility warning step.\n///\n/// The \"default\" for skip-leak-check corresponds to the current\n/// behavior (do not skip the leak check) -- not the behavior we are\n/// transitioning into.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Default)]\npub enum SkipLeakCheck {\n    Yes,\n    #[default]\n    No,\n}\n\nimpl SkipLeakCheck {\n    fn is_yes(self) -> bool {\n        self == SkipLeakCheck::Yes\n    }\n}\n\n/// The mode that trait queries run in.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Debug)]\npub enum TraitQueryMode {\n    /// Standard/un-canonicalized queries get accurate\n    /// spans etc. passed in and hence can do reasonable\n    /// error reporting on their own.\n    Standard,\n    /// Canonical queries get dummy spans and hence\n    /// must generally propagate errors to\n    /// pre-canonicalization callsites.\n    Canonical,\n}\n\n/// Creates predicate obligations from the generic bounds.\n#[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip(cause, param_env))]\npub fn predicates_for_generics<'tcx>(\n    cause: impl Fn(usize, Span) -> ObligationCause<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    generic_bounds: ty::InstantiatedPredicates<'tcx>,\n) -> impl Iterator<Item = PredicateObligation<'tcx>> {\n    generic_bounds.into_iter().enumerate().map(move |(idx, (clause, span))| Obligation {\n        cause: cause(idx, span),\n        recursion_depth: 0,\n        param_env,\n        predicate: clause.as_predicate(),\n    })\n}\n\n/// Determines whether the type `ty` is known to meet `bound` and\n/// returns true if so. Returns false if `ty` either does not meet\n/// `bound` or is not known to meet bound (note that this is\n/// conservative towards *no impl*, which is the opposite of the\n/// `evaluate` methods).\npub fn type_known_to_meet_bound_modulo_regions<'tcx>(\n    infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n    def_id: DefId,\n) -> bool {\n    let trait_ref = ty::TraitRef::new(infcx.tcx, def_id, [ty]);\n    pred_known_to_hold_modulo_regions(infcx, param_env, trait_ref)\n}\n\n/// FIXME(@lcnr): this function doesn't seem right and shouldn't exist?\n///\n/// Ping me on zulip if you want to use this method and need help with finding\n/// an appropriate replacement.\n#[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip(infcx, param_env, pred), ret)]\nfn pred_known_to_hold_modulo_regions<'tcx>(\n    infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    pred: impl Upcast<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>,\n) -> bool {\n    let obligation = Obligation::new(infcx.tcx, ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, pred);\n\n    let result = infcx.evaluate_obligation_no_overflow(&obligation);\n    debug!(?result);\n\n    if result.must_apply_modulo_regions() {\n        true\n    } else if result.may_apply() && !infcx.next_trait_solver() {\n        // Sometimes obligations are ambiguous because the recursive evaluator\n        // is not smart enough, so we fall back to fulfillment when we're not certain\n        // that an obligation holds or not. Even still, we must make sure that\n        // the we do no inference in the process of checking this obligation.\n        let goal = infcx.resolve_vars_if_possible((obligation.predicate, obligation.param_env));\n        infcx.probe(|_| {\n            let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new(infcx);\n            ocx.register_obligation(obligation);\n\n            let errors = ocx.evaluate_obligations_error_on_ambiguity();\n            match errors.as_slice() {\n                // Only known to hold if we did no inference.\n                [] => infcx.resolve_vars_if_possible(goal) == goal,\n\n                errors => {\n                    debug!(?errors);\n                    false\n                }\n            }\n        })\n    } else {\n        false\n    }\n}\n\n#[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip(tcx, elaborated_env))]\nfn do_normalize_predicates<'tcx>(\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n    cause: ObligationCause<'tcx>,\n    elaborated_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    predicates: Vec<ty::Clause<'tcx>>,\n) -> Result<Vec<ty::Clause<'tcx>>, ErrorGuaranteed> {\n    let span = cause.span;\n\n    // FIXME. We should really... do something with these region\n    // obligations. But this call just continues the older\n    // behavior (i.e., doesn't cause any new bugs), and it would\n    // take some further refactoring to actually solve them. In\n    // particular, we would have to handle implied bounds\n    // properly, and that code is currently largely confined to\n    // regionck (though I made some efforts to extract it\n    // out). -nmatsakis\n    //\n    // @arielby: In any case, these obligations are checked\n    // by wfcheck anyway, so I'm not sure we have to check\n    // them here too, and we will remove this function when\n    // we move over to lazy normalization *anyway*.\n    let infcx = tcx.infer_ctxt().ignoring_regions().build(TypingMode::non_body_analysis());\n    let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new_with_diagnostics(&infcx);\n    let predicates = ocx.normalize(&cause, elaborated_env, predicates);\n\n    let errors = ocx.evaluate_obligations_error_on_ambiguity();\n    if !errors.is_empty() {\n        let reported = infcx.err_ctxt().report_fulfillment_errors(errors);\n        return Err(reported);\n    }\n\n    debug!(\"do_normalize_predicates: normalized predicates = {:?}\", predicates);\n\n    // We can use the `elaborated_env` here; the region code only\n    // cares about declarations like `'a: 'b`.\n    // FIXME: It's very weird that we ignore region obligations but apparently\n    // still need to use `resolve_regions` as we need the resolved regions in\n    // the normalized predicates.\n    let errors = infcx.resolve_regions(cause.body_id, elaborated_env, []);\n    if !errors.is_empty() {\n        tcx.dcx().span_delayed_bug(\n            span,\n            format!(\"failed region resolution while normalizing {elaborated_env:?}: {errors:?}\"),\n        );\n    }\n\n    match infcx.fully_resolve(predicates) {\n        Ok(predicates) => Ok(predicates),\n        Err(fixup_err) => {\n            // If we encounter a fixup error, it means that some type\n            // variable wound up unconstrained. I actually don't know\n            // if this can happen, and I certainly don't expect it to\n            // happen often, but if it did happen it probably\n            // represents a legitimate failure due to some kind of\n            // unconstrained variable.\n            //\n            // @lcnr: Let's still ICE here for now. I want a test case\n            // for that.\n            span_bug!(\n                span,\n                \"inference variables in normalized parameter environment: {}\",\n                fixup_err\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// FIXME: this is gonna need to be removed ...\n/// Normalizes the parameter environment, reporting errors if they occur.\n#[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip(tcx))]\npub fn normalize_param_env_or_error<'tcx>(\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n    unnormalized_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    cause: ObligationCause<'tcx>,\n) -> ty::ParamEnv<'tcx> {\n    // I'm not wild about reporting errors here; I'd prefer to\n    // have the errors get reported at a defined place (e.g.,\n    // during typeck). Instead I have all parameter\n    // environments, in effect, going through this function\n    // and hence potentially reporting errors. This ensures of\n    // course that we never forget to normalize (the\n    // alternative seemed like it would involve a lot of\n    // manual invocations of this fn -- and then we'd have to\n    // deal with the errors at each of those sites).\n    //\n    // In any case, in practice, typeck constructs all the\n    // parameter environments once for every fn as it goes,\n    // and errors will get reported then; so outside of type inference we\n    // can be sure that no errors should occur.\n    let mut predicates: Vec<_> = util::elaborate(\n        tcx,\n        unnormalized_env.caller_bounds().into_iter().map(|predicate| {\n            if tcx.features().generic_const_exprs() || tcx.next_trait_solver_globally() {\n                return predicate;\n            }\n\n            struct ConstNormalizer<'tcx>(TyCtxt<'tcx>);\n\n            impl<'tcx> TypeFolder<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for ConstNormalizer<'tcx> {\n                fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n                    self.0\n                }\n\n                fn fold_const(&mut self, c: ty::Const<'tcx>) -> ty::Const<'tcx> {\n                    // FIXME(return_type_notation): track binders in this normalizer, as\n                    // `ty::Const::normalize` can only work with properly preserved binders.\n\n                    if c.has_escaping_bound_vars() {\n                        return ty::Const::new_misc_error(self.0);\n                    }\n\n                    // While it is pretty sus to be evaluating things with an empty param env, it\n                    // should actually be okay since without `feature(generic_const_exprs)` the only\n                    // const arguments that have a non-empty param env are array repeat counts. These\n                    // do not appear in the type system though.\n                    if let ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) = c.kind()\n                        && self.0.def_kind(uv.def) == DefKind::AnonConst\n                    {\n                        let infcx = self.0.infer_ctxt().build(TypingMode::non_body_analysis());\n                        let c = evaluate_const(&infcx, c, ty::ParamEnv::empty());\n                        // We should never wind up with any `infcx` local state when normalizing anon consts\n                        // under min const generics.\n                        assert!(!c.has_infer() && !c.has_placeholders());\n                        return c;\n                    }\n\n                    c\n                }\n            }\n\n            // This whole normalization step is a hack to work around the fact that\n            // `normalize_param_env_or_error` is fundamentally broken from using an\n            // unnormalized param env with a trait solver that expects the param env\n            // to be normalized.\n            //\n            // When normalizing the param env we can end up evaluating obligations\n            // that have been normalized but can only be proven via a where clause\n            // which is still in its unnormalized form. example:\n            //\n            // Attempting to prove `T: Trait<<u8 as Identity>::Assoc>` in a param env\n            // with a `T: Trait<<u8 as Identity>::Assoc>` where clause will fail because\n            // we first normalize obligations before proving them so we end up proving\n            // `T: Trait<u8>`. Since lazy normalization is not implemented equating `u8`\n            // with `<u8 as Identity>::Assoc` fails outright so we incorrectly believe that\n            // we cannot prove `T: Trait<u8>`.\n            //\n            // The same thing is true for const generics- attempting to prove\n            // `T: Trait<ConstKind::Unevaluated(...)>` with the same thing as a where clauses\n            // will fail. After normalization we may be attempting to prove `T: Trait<4>` with\n            // the unnormalized where clause `T: Trait<ConstKind::Unevaluated(...)>`. In order\n            // for the obligation to hold `4` must be equal to `ConstKind::Unevaluated(...)`\n            // but as we do not have lazy norm implemented, equating the two consts fails outright.\n            //\n            // Ideally we would not normalize consts here at all but it is required for backwards\n            // compatibility. Eventually when lazy norm is implemented this can just be removed.\n            // We do not normalize types here as there is no backwards compatibility requirement\n            // for us to do so.\n            predicate.fold_with(&mut ConstNormalizer(tcx))\n        }),\n    )\n    .collect();\n\n    debug!(\"normalize_param_env_or_error: elaborated-predicates={:?}\", predicates);\n\n    let elaborated_env = ty::ParamEnv::new(tcx.mk_clauses(&predicates));\n    if !elaborated_env.has_aliases() {\n        return elaborated_env;\n    }\n\n    // HACK: we are trying to normalize the param-env inside *itself*. The problem is that\n    // normalization expects its param-env to be already normalized, which means we have\n    // a circularity.\n    //\n    // The way we handle this is by normalizing the param-env inside an unnormalized version\n    // of the param-env, which means that if the param-env contains unnormalized projections,\n    // we'll have some normalization failures. This is unfortunate.\n    //\n    // Lazy normalization would basically handle this by treating just the\n    // normalizing-a-trait-ref-requires-itself cycles as evaluation failures.\n    //\n    // Inferred outlives bounds can create a lot of `TypeOutlives` predicates for associated\n    // types, so to make the situation less bad, we normalize all the predicates *but*\n    // the `TypeOutlives` predicates first inside the unnormalized parameter environment, and\n    // then we normalize the `TypeOutlives` bounds inside the normalized parameter environment.\n    //\n    // This works fairly well because trait matching does not actually care about param-env\n    // TypeOutlives predicates - these are normally used by regionck.\n    let outlives_predicates: Vec<_> = predicates\n        .extract_if(.., |predicate| {\n            matches!(predicate.kind().skip_binder(), ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(..))\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    debug!(\n        \"normalize_param_env_or_error: predicates=(non-outlives={:?}, outlives={:?})\",\n        predicates, outlives_predicates\n    );\n    let Ok(non_outlives_predicates) =\n        do_normalize_predicates(tcx, cause.clone(), elaborated_env, predicates)\n    else {\n        // An unnormalized env is better than nothing.\n        debug!(\"normalize_param_env_or_error: errored resolving non-outlives predicates\");\n        return elaborated_env;\n    };\n\n    debug!(\"normalize_param_env_or_error: non-outlives predicates={:?}\", non_outlives_predicates);\n\n    // Not sure whether it is better to include the unnormalized TypeOutlives predicates\n    // here. I believe they should not matter, because we are ignoring TypeOutlives param-env\n    // predicates here anyway. Keeping them here anyway because it seems safer.\n    let outlives_env = non_outlives_predicates.iter().chain(&outlives_predicates).cloned();\n    let outlives_env = ty::ParamEnv::new(tcx.mk_clauses_from_iter(outlives_env));\n    let Ok(outlives_predicates) =\n        do_normalize_predicates(tcx, cause, outlives_env, outlives_predicates)\n    else {\n        // An unnormalized env is better than nothing.\n        debug!(\"normalize_param_env_or_error: errored resolving outlives predicates\");\n        return elaborated_env;\n    };\n    debug!(\"normalize_param_env_or_error: outlives predicates={:?}\", outlives_predicates);\n\n    let mut predicates = non_outlives_predicates;\n    predicates.extend(outlives_predicates);\n    debug!(\"normalize_param_env_or_error: final predicates={:?}\", predicates);\n    ty::ParamEnv::new(tcx.mk_clauses(&predicates))\n}\n\n/// Deeply normalize the param env using the next solver ignoring\n/// region errors.\n///\n/// FIXME(-Zhigher-ranked-assumptions): this is a hack to work around\n/// the fact that we don't support placeholder assumptions right now\n/// and is necessary for `compare_method_predicate_entailment`, see the\n/// use of this function for more info. We should remove this once we\n/// have proper support for implied bounds on binders.\n#[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip(tcx))]\npub fn deeply_normalize_param_env_ignoring_regions<'tcx>(\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n    unnormalized_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    cause: ObligationCause<'tcx>,\n) -> ty::ParamEnv<'tcx> {\n    let predicates: Vec<_> =\n        util::elaborate(tcx, unnormalized_env.caller_bounds().into_iter()).collect();\n\n    debug!(\"normalize_param_env_or_error: elaborated-predicates={:?}\", predicates);\n\n    let elaborated_env = ty::ParamEnv::new(tcx.mk_clauses(&predicates));\n    if !elaborated_env.has_aliases() {\n        return elaborated_env;\n    }\n\n    let span = cause.span;\n    let infcx = tcx\n        .infer_ctxt()\n        .with_next_trait_solver(true)\n        .ignoring_regions()\n        .build(TypingMode::non_body_analysis());\n    let predicates = match crate::solve::deeply_normalize::<_, FulfillmentError<'tcx>>(\n        infcx.at(&cause, elaborated_env),\n        predicates,\n    ) {\n        Ok(predicates) => predicates,\n        Err(errors) => {\n            infcx.err_ctxt().report_fulfillment_errors(errors);\n            // An unnormalized env is better than nothing.\n            debug!(\"normalize_param_env_or_error: errored resolving predicates\");\n            return elaborated_env;\n        }\n    };\n\n    debug!(\"do_normalize_predicates: normalized predicates = {:?}\", predicates);\n    // FIXME(-Zhigher-ranked-assumptions): We're ignoring region errors for now.\n    // There're placeholder constraints `leaking` out.\n    // See the fixme in the enclosing function's docs for more.\n    let _errors = infcx.resolve_regions(cause.body_id, elaborated_env, []);\n\n    let predicates = match infcx.fully_resolve(predicates) {\n        Ok(predicates) => predicates,\n        Err(fixup_err) => {\n            span_bug!(\n                span,\n                \"inference variables in normalized parameter environment: {}\",\n                fixup_err\n            )\n        }\n    };\n    debug!(\"normalize_param_env_or_error: final predicates={:?}\", predicates);\n    ty::ParamEnv::new(tcx.mk_clauses(&predicates))\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum EvaluateConstErr {\n    /// The constant being evaluated was either a generic parameter or inference variable, *or*,\n    /// some unevaluated constant with either generic parameters or inference variables in its\n    /// generic arguments.\n    HasGenericsOrInfers,\n    /// The type this constant evaluated to is not valid for use in const generics. This should\n    /// always result in an error when checking the constant is correctly typed for the parameter\n    /// it is an argument to, so a bug is delayed when encountering this.\n    InvalidConstParamTy(ErrorGuaranteed),\n    /// CTFE failed to evaluate the constant in some unrecoverable way (e.g. encountered a `panic!`).\n    /// This is also used when the constant was already tainted by error.\n    EvaluationFailure(ErrorGuaranteed),\n}\n\n// FIXME(BoxyUwU): Private this once we `generic_const_exprs` isn't doing its own normalization routine\n// FIXME(generic_const_exprs): Consider accepting a `ty::UnevaluatedConst` when we are not rolling our own\n// normalization scheme\n/// Evaluates a type system constant returning a `ConstKind::Error` in cases where CTFE failed and\n/// returning the passed in constant if it was not fully concrete (i.e. depended on generic parameters\n/// or inference variables)\n///\n/// You should not call this function unless you are implementing normalization itself. Prefer to use\n/// `normalize_erasing_regions` or the `normalize` functions on `ObligationCtxt`/`FnCtxt`/`InferCtxt`.\npub fn evaluate_const<'tcx>(\n    infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n    ct: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n) -> ty::Const<'tcx> {\n    match try_evaluate_const(infcx, ct, param_env) {\n        Ok(ct) => ct,\n        Err(EvaluateConstErr::EvaluationFailure(e) | EvaluateConstErr::InvalidConstParamTy(e)) => {\n            ty::Const::new_error(infcx.tcx, e)\n        }\n        Err(EvaluateConstErr::HasGenericsOrInfers) => ct,\n    }\n}\n\n// FIXME(BoxyUwU): Private this once we `generic_const_exprs` isn't doing its own normalization routine\n// FIXME(generic_const_exprs): Consider accepting a `ty::UnevaluatedConst` when we are not rolling our own\n// normalization scheme\n/// Evaluates a type system constant making sure to not allow constants that depend on generic parameters\n/// or inference variables to succeed in evaluating.\n///\n/// You should not call this function unless you are implementing normalization itself. Prefer to use\n/// `normalize_erasing_regions` or the `normalize` functions on `ObligationCtxt`/`FnCtxt`/`InferCtxt`.\n#[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip(infcx), ret)]\npub fn try_evaluate_const<'tcx>(\n    infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n    ct: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n) -> Result<ty::Const<'tcx>, EvaluateConstErr> {\n    let tcx = infcx.tcx;\n    let ct = infcx.resolve_vars_if_possible(ct);\n    debug!(?ct);\n\n    match ct.kind() {\n        ty::ConstKind::Value(..) => Ok(ct),\n        ty::ConstKind::Error(e) => Err(EvaluateConstErr::EvaluationFailure(e)),\n        ty::ConstKind::Param(_)\n        | ty::ConstKind::Infer(_)\n        | ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _)\n        | ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_)\n        | ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => Err(EvaluateConstErr::HasGenericsOrInfers),\n        ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n            let opt_anon_const_kind =\n                (tcx.def_kind(uv.def) == DefKind::AnonConst).then(|| tcx.anon_const_kind(uv.def));\n\n            // Postpone evaluation of constants that depend on generic parameters or\n            // inference variables.\n            //\n            // We use `TypingMode::PostAnalysis` here which is not *technically* correct\n            // to be revealing opaque types here as borrowcheck has not run yet. However,\n            // CTFE itself uses `TypingMode::PostAnalysis` unconditionally even during\n            // typeck and not doing so has a lot of (undesirable) fallout (#101478, #119821).\n            // As a result we always use a revealed env when resolving the instance to evaluate.\n            //\n            // FIXME: `const_eval_resolve_for_typeck` should probably just modify the env itself\n            // instead of having this logic here\n            let (args, typing_env) = match opt_anon_const_kind {\n                // We handle `generic_const_exprs` separately as reasonable ways of handling constants in the type system\n                // completely fall apart under `generic_const_exprs` and makes this whole function Really hard to reason\n                // about if you have to consider gce whatsoever.\n                Some(ty::AnonConstKind::GCE) => {\n                    if uv.has_non_region_infer() || uv.has_non_region_param() {\n                        // `feature(generic_const_exprs)` causes anon consts to inherit all parent generics. This can cause\n                        // inference variables and generic parameters to show up in `ty::Const` even though the anon const\n                        // does not actually make use of them. We handle this case specially and attempt to evaluate anyway.\n                        match tcx.thir_abstract_const(uv.def) {\n                            Ok(Some(ct)) => {\n                                let ct = tcx.expand_abstract_consts(ct.instantiate(tcx, uv.args));\n                                if let Err(e) = ct.error_reported() {\n                                    return Err(EvaluateConstErr::EvaluationFailure(e));\n                                } else if ct.has_non_region_infer() || ct.has_non_region_param() {\n                                    // If the anon const *does* actually use generic parameters or inference variables from\n                                    // the generic arguments provided for it, then we should *not* attempt to evaluate it.\n                                    return Err(EvaluateConstErr::HasGenericsOrInfers);\n                                } else {\n                                    let args =\n                                        replace_param_and_infer_args_with_placeholder(tcx, uv.args);\n                                    let typing_env = infcx\n                                        .typing_env(tcx.erase_and_anonymize_regions(param_env))\n                                        .with_post_analysis_normalized(tcx);\n                                    (args, typing_env)\n                                }\n                            }\n                            Err(_) | Ok(None) => {\n                                let args = GenericArgs::identity_for_item(tcx, uv.def);\n                                let typing_env = ty::TypingEnv::post_analysis(tcx, uv.def);\n                                (args, typing_env)\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        let typing_env = infcx\n                            .typing_env(tcx.erase_and_anonymize_regions(param_env))\n                            .with_post_analysis_normalized(tcx);\n                        (uv.args, typing_env)\n                    }\n                }\n                Some(ty::AnonConstKind::RepeatExprCount) => {\n                    if uv.has_non_region_infer() {\n                        // Diagnostics will sometimes replace the identity args of anon consts in\n                        // array repeat expr counts with inference variables so we have to handle this\n                        // even though it is not something we should ever actually encounter.\n                        //\n                        // Array repeat expr counts are allowed to syntactically use generic parameters\n                        // but must not actually depend on them in order to evalaute successfully. This means\n                        // that it is actually fine to evalaute them in their own environment rather than with\n                        // the actually provided generic arguments.\n                        tcx.dcx().delayed_bug(\"AnonConst with infer args but no error reported\");\n                    }\n\n                    // The generic args of repeat expr counts under `min_const_generics` are not supposed to\n                    // affect evaluation of the constant as this would make it a \"truly\" generic const arg.\n                    // To prevent this we discard all the generic arguments and evalaute with identity args\n                    // and in its own environment instead of the current environment we are normalizing in.\n                    let args = GenericArgs::identity_for_item(tcx, uv.def);\n                    let typing_env = ty::TypingEnv::post_analysis(tcx, uv.def);\n\n                    (args, typing_env)\n                }\n                Some(ty::AnonConstKind::MCG) | Some(ty::AnonConstKind::NonTypeSystem) | None => {\n                    // We are only dealing with \"truly\" generic/uninferred constants here:\n                    // - GCEConsts have been handled separately\n                    // - Repeat expr count back compat consts have also been handled separately\n                    // So we are free to simply defer evaluation here.\n                    //\n                    // FIXME: This assumes that `args` are normalized which is not necessarily true\n                    //\n                    // Const patterns are converted to type system constants before being\n                    // evaluated. However, we don't care about them here as pattern evaluation\n                    // logic does not go through type system normalization. If it did this would\n                    // be a backwards compatibility problem as we do not enforce \"syntactic\" non-\n                    // usage of generic parameters like we do here.\n                    if uv.args.has_non_region_param() || uv.args.has_non_region_infer() {\n                        return Err(EvaluateConstErr::HasGenericsOrInfers);\n                    }\n\n                    // Since there is no generic parameter, we can just drop the environment\n                    // to prevent query cycle.\n                    let typing_env = ty::TypingEnv::fully_monomorphized();\n\n                    (uv.args, typing_env)\n                }\n            };\n\n            let uv = ty::UnevaluatedConst::new(uv.def, args);\n            let erased_uv = tcx.erase_and_anonymize_regions(uv);\n\n            use rustc_middle::mir::interpret::ErrorHandled;\n            // FIXME: `def_span` will point at the definition of this const; ideally, we'd point at\n            // where it gets used as a const generic.\n            match tcx.const_eval_resolve_for_typeck(typing_env, erased_uv, tcx.def_span(uv.def)) {\n                Ok(Ok(val)) => Ok(ty::Const::new_value(\n                    tcx,\n                    val,\n                    tcx.type_of(uv.def).instantiate(tcx, uv.args),\n                )),\n                Ok(Err(_)) => {\n                    let e = tcx.dcx().delayed_bug(\n                        \"Type system constant with non valtree'able type evaluated but no error emitted\",\n                    );\n                    Err(EvaluateConstErr::InvalidConstParamTy(e))\n                }\n                Err(ErrorHandled::Reported(info, _)) => {\n                    Err(EvaluateConstErr::EvaluationFailure(info.into()))\n                }\n                Err(ErrorHandled::TooGeneric(_)) => Err(EvaluateConstErr::HasGenericsOrInfers),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Replaces args that reference param or infer variables with suitable\n/// placeholders. This function is meant to remove these param and infer\n/// args when they're not actually needed to evaluate a constant.\nfn replace_param_and_infer_args_with_placeholder<'tcx>(\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n    args: GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n) -> GenericArgsRef<'tcx> {\n    struct ReplaceParamAndInferWithPlaceholder<'tcx> {\n        tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n        idx: ty::BoundVar,\n    }\n\n    impl<'tcx> TypeFolder<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for ReplaceParamAndInferWithPlaceholder<'tcx> {\n        fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n            self.tcx\n        }\n\n        fn fold_ty(&mut self, t: Ty<'tcx>) -> Ty<'tcx> {\n            if let ty::Infer(_) = t.kind() {\n                let idx = self.idx;\n                self.idx += 1;\n                Ty::new_placeholder(\n                    self.tcx,\n                    ty::PlaceholderType {\n                        universe: ty::UniverseIndex::ROOT,\n                        bound: ty::BoundTy { var: idx, kind: ty::BoundTyKind::Anon },\n                    },\n                )\n            } else {\n                t.super_fold_with(self)\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn fold_const(&mut self, c: ty::Const<'tcx>) -> ty::Const<'tcx> {\n            if let ty::ConstKind::Infer(_) = c.kind() {\n                let idx = self.idx;\n                self.idx += 1;\n                ty::Const::new_placeholder(\n                    self.tcx,\n                    ty::PlaceholderConst {\n                        universe: ty::UniverseIndex::ROOT,\n                        bound: ty::BoundConst { var: idx },\n                    },\n                )\n            } else {\n                c.super_fold_with(self)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    args.fold_with(&mut ReplaceParamAndInferWithPlaceholder { tcx, idx: ty::BoundVar::ZERO })\n}\n\n/// Normalizes the predicates and checks whether they hold in an empty environment. If this\n/// returns true, then either normalize encountered an error or one of the predicates did not\n/// hold. Used when creating vtables to check for unsatisfiable methods. This should not be\n/// used during analysis.\npub fn impossible_predicates<'tcx>(tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, predicates: Vec<ty::Clause<'tcx>>) -> bool {\n    debug!(\"impossible_predicates(predicates={:?})\", predicates);\n    let (infcx, param_env) = tcx\n        .infer_ctxt()\n        .with_next_trait_solver(true)\n        .build_with_typing_env(ty::TypingEnv::fully_monomorphized());\n\n    let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new(&infcx);\n    let predicates = ocx.normalize(&ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, predicates);\n    for predicate in predicates {\n        let obligation = Obligation::new(tcx, ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, predicate);\n        ocx.register_obligation(obligation);\n    }\n\n    // Use `try_evaluate_obligations` to only return impossible for true errors,\n    // and not ambiguities or overflows. Since the new trait solver forces\n    // some currently undetected overlap between `dyn Trait: Trait` built-in\n    // vs user-written impls to AMBIGUOUS, this may return ambiguity even\n    // with no infer vars. There may also be ways to encounter ambiguity due\n    // to post-mono overflow.\n    let true_errors = ocx.try_evaluate_obligations();\n    if !true_errors.is_empty() {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    false\n}\n\nfn instantiate_and_check_impossible_predicates<'tcx>(\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n    key: (DefId, GenericArgsRef<'tcx>),\n) -> bool {\n    debug!(\"instantiate_and_check_impossible_predicates(key={:?})\", key);\n\n    let mut predicates = tcx.predicates_of(key.0).instantiate(tcx, key.1).predicates;\n\n    // Specifically check trait fulfillment to avoid an error when trying to resolve\n    // associated items.\n    if let Some(trait_def_id) = tcx.trait_of_assoc(key.0) {\n        let trait_ref = ty::TraitRef::from_assoc(tcx, trait_def_id, key.1);\n        predicates.push(trait_ref.upcast(tcx));\n    }\n\n    predicates.retain(|predicate| !predicate.has_param());\n    let result = impossible_predicates(tcx, predicates);\n\n    debug!(\"instantiate_and_check_impossible_predicates(key={:?}) = {:?}\", key, result);\n    result\n}\n\n/// Checks whether a trait's associated item is impossible to reference on a given impl.\n///\n/// This only considers predicates that reference the impl's generics, and not\n/// those that reference the method's generics.\nfn is_impossible_associated_item(\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'_>,\n    (impl_def_id, trait_item_def_id): (DefId, DefId),\n) -> bool {\n    struct ReferencesOnlyParentGenerics<'tcx> {\n        tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n        generics: &'tcx ty::Generics,\n        trait_item_def_id: DefId,\n    }\n    impl<'tcx> ty::TypeVisitor<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for ReferencesOnlyParentGenerics<'tcx> {\n        type Result = ControlFlow<()>;\n        fn visit_ty(&mut self, t: Ty<'tcx>) -> Self::Result {\n            // If this is a parameter from the trait item's own generics, then bail\n            if let ty::Param(param) = *t.kind()\n                && let param_def_id = self.generics.type_param(param, self.tcx).def_id\n                && self.tcx.parent(param_def_id) == self.trait_item_def_id\n            {\n                return ControlFlow::Break(());\n            }\n            t.super_visit_with(self)\n        }\n        fn visit_region(&mut self, r: ty::Region<'tcx>) -> Self::Result {\n            if let ty::ReEarlyParam(param) = r.kind()\n                && let param_def_id = self.generics.region_param(param, self.tcx).def_id\n                && self.tcx.parent(param_def_id) == self.trait_item_def_id\n            {\n                return ControlFlow::Break(());\n            }\n            ControlFlow::Continue(())\n        }\n        fn visit_const(&mut self, ct: ty::Const<'tcx>) -> Self::Result {\n            if let ty::ConstKind::Param(param) = ct.kind()\n                && let param_def_id = self.generics.const_param(param, self.tcx).def_id\n                && self.tcx.parent(param_def_id) == self.trait_item_def_id\n            {\n                return ControlFlow::Break(());\n            }\n            ct.super_visit_with(self)\n        }\n    }\n\n    let generics = tcx.generics_of(trait_item_def_id);\n    let predicates = tcx.predicates_of(trait_item_def_id);\n\n    // Be conservative in cases where we have `W<T: ?Sized>` and a method like `Self: Sized`,\n    // since that method *may* have some substitutions where the predicates hold.\n    //\n    // This replicates the logic we use in coherence.\n    let infcx = tcx\n        .infer_ctxt()\n        .ignoring_regions()\n        .with_next_trait_solver(true)\n        .build(TypingMode::Coherence);\n    let param_env = ty::ParamEnv::empty();\n    let fresh_args = infcx.fresh_args_for_item(tcx.def_span(impl_def_id), impl_def_id);\n\n    let impl_trait_ref = tcx.impl_trait_ref(impl_def_id).instantiate(tcx, fresh_args);\n\n    let mut visitor = ReferencesOnlyParentGenerics { tcx, generics, trait_item_def_id };\n    let predicates_for_trait = predicates.predicates.iter().filter_map(|(pred, span)| {\n        pred.visit_with(&mut visitor).is_continue().then(|| {\n            Obligation::new(\n                tcx,\n                ObligationCause::dummy_with_span(*span),\n                param_env,\n                ty::EarlyBinder::bind(*pred).instantiate(tcx, impl_trait_ref.args),\n            )\n        })\n    });\n\n    let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new(&infcx);\n    ocx.register_obligations(predicates_for_trait);\n    !ocx.try_evaluate_obligations().is_empty()\n}\n\npub fn provide(providers: &mut Providers) {\n    dyn_compatibility::provide(providers);\n    vtable::provide(providers);\n    *providers = Providers {\n        specialization_graph_of: specialize::specialization_graph_provider,\n        specializes: specialize::specializes,\n        specialization_enabled_in: specialize::specialization_enabled_in,\n        instantiate_and_check_impossible_predicates,\n        is_impossible_associated_item,\n        ..*providers\n    };\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":115,"oldLines":7,"newStart":115,"newLines":7,"lines":["     Cycle(PredicateObligations<'tcx>),","     Select(SelectionError<'tcx>),"," try_from_target_usize(val: u64) -> Result<Self, Error> {\n        with(|cx| cx.try_new_ty_const_uint(val.into(), UintTy::Usize))\n    }\n\n    /// Try to evaluate to a target `usize`.\n    pub fn eval_target_usize(&self) -> Result<u64, Error> {\n        with(|cx| cx.eval_target_usize_ty(self))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum TyConstKind {\n    Param(ParamConst),\n    Bound(DebruijnIndex, BoundVar),\n    Unevaluated(ConstDef, GenericArgs),\n\n    // FIXME: These should be a valtree\n    Value(Ty, Allocation),\n    ZSTValue(Ty),\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash)]\npub struct TyConstId(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\n\n/// Represents a constant in MIR\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct MirConst {\n    /// The constant kind.\n    pub(crate) kind: ConstantKind,\n    /// The constant type.\n    pub(crate) ty: Ty,\n    /// Used for internal tracking of the internal constant.\n    pub id: MirConstId,\n}\n\nimpl MirConst {\n    /// Build a constant. Note that this should only be used by the compiler.\n    pub fn new(kind: ConstantKind, ty: Ty, id: MirConstId) -> MirConst {\n        MirConst { kind, ty, id }\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve the constant kind.\n    pub fn kind(&self) -> &ConstantKind {\n        &self.kind\n    }\n\n    /// Get the constant type.\n    pub fn ty(&self) -> Ty {\n        self.ty\n    }\n\n    /// Try to evaluate to a target `usize`.\n    pub fn eval_target_usize(&self) -> Result<u64, Error> {\n        with(|cx| cx.eval_target_usize(self))\n    }\n\n    /// Create a constant that represents a new zero-sized constant of type T.\n    /// Fails if the type is not a ZST or if it doesn't have a known size.\n    pub fn try_new_zero_sized(ty: Ty) -> Result<MirConst, Error> {\n        with(|cx| cx.try_new_const_zst(ty))\n    }\n\n    /// Build a new constant that represents the given string.\n    ///\n    /// Note that there is no guarantee today about duplication of the same constant.\n    /// I.e.: Calling this function multiple times with the same argument may or may not return\n    /// the same allocation.\n    pub fn from_str(value: &str) -> MirConst {\n        with(|cx| cx.new_const_str(value))\n    }\n\n    /// Build a new constant that represents the given boolean value.\n    pub fn from_bool(value: bool) -> MirConst {\n        with(|cx| cx.new_const_bool(value))\n    }\n\n    /// Build a new constant that represents the given unsigned integer.\n    pub fn try_from_uint(value: u128, uint_ty: UintTy) -> Result<MirConst, Error> {\n        with(|cx| cx.try_new_const_uint(value, uint_ty))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct MirConstId(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\n\ntype Ident = Opaque;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct Region {\n    pub kind: RegionKind,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum RegionKind {\n    ReEarlyParam(EarlyParamRegion),\n    ReBound(DebruijnIndex, BoundRegion),\n    ReStatic,\n    RePlaceholder(Placeholder<BoundRegion>),\n    ReErased,\n}\n\npub(crate) type DebruijnIndex = u32;\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct EarlyParamRegion {\n    pub index: u32,\n    pub name: Symbol,\n}\n\npub(crate) type BoundVar = u32;\n\n#[derive(Clone, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct Placeholder<T> {\n    pub universe: UniverseIndex,\n    pub bound: T,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct Span(usize, ThreadLocalIndex);\n\nimpl Debug for Span {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"Span\")\n            .field(\"id\", &self.0)\n            .field(\"repr\", &with(|cx| cx.span_to_string(*self)))\n            .finish()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Span {\n    /// Return filename for diagnostic purposes\n    pub fn get_filename(&self) -> Filename {\n        with(|c| c.get_filename(self))\n    }\n\n    /// Return lines that correspond to this `Span`\n    pub fn get_lines(&self) -> LineInfo {\n        with(|c| c.get_lines(self))\n    }\n\n    /// Return the span location to be printed in diagnostic messages.\n    ///\n    /// This may leak local file paths and should not be used to build artifacts that may be\n    /// distributed.\n    pub fn diagnostic(&self) -> String {\n        with(|c| c.span_to_string(*self))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Serialize)]\n/// Information you get from `Span` in a struct form.\n/// Line and col start from 1.\npub struct LineInfo {\n    pub start_line: usize,\n    pub start_col: usize,\n    pub end_line: usize,\n    pub end_col: usize,\n}\n\nimpl LineInfo {\n    pub fn from(lines: (usize, usize, usize, usize)) -> Self {\n        LineInfo { start_line: lines.0, start_col: lines.1, end_line: lines.2, end_col: lines.3 }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum TyKind {\n    RigidTy(RigidTy),\n    Alias(AliasKind, AliasTy),\n    Param(ParamTy),\n    Bound(usize, BoundTy),\n}\n\nimpl TyKind {\n    pub fn rigid(&self) -> Option<&RigidTy> {\n        if let TyKind::RigidTy(inner) = self { Some(inner) } else { None }\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_unit(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Tuple(data)) if data.is_empty())\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_bool(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Bool))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_char(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Char))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_trait(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Dynamic(_, _)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_enum(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Enum)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_struct(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Struct)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_union(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.kind() == AdtKind::Union)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_adt(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_ref(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_fn(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_fn_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_primitive(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self,\n            TyKind::RigidTy(\n                RigidTy::Bool\n                    | RigidTy::Char\n                    | RigidTy::Int(_)\n                    | RigidTy::Uint(_)\n                    | RigidTy::Float(_)\n            )\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_float(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Float(_)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_integral(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(_) | RigidTy::Uint(_)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_numeric(&self) -> bool {\n        self.is_integral() || self.is_float()\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_signed(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Int(_)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_str(&self) -> bool {\n        *self == TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Str)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_cstr(&self) -> bool {\n        let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) = self else {\n            return false;\n        };\n        with(|cx| cx.adt_is_cstr(*def))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_slice(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Slice(_)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_array(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Array(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_mutable_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self,\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(_, Mutability::Mut))\n                | TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Ref(_, _, Mutability::Mut))\n        )\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_raw_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::RawPtr(..)))\n    }\n\n    /// Tests if this is any kind of primitive pointer type (reference, raw pointer, fn pointer).\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_any_ptr(&self) -> bool {\n        self.is_ref() || self.is_raw_ptr() || self.is_fn_ptr()\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_coroutine(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Coroutine(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_closure(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(..)))\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_box(&self) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) => def.is_box(),\n            _ => false,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn is_simd(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Adt(def, _)) if def.is_simd())\n    }\n\n    pub fn trait_principal(&self) -> Option<Binder<ExistentialTraitRef>> {\n        if let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Dynamic(predicates, _)) = self {\n            if let Some(Binder { value: ExistentialPredicate::Trait(trait_ref), bound_vars }) =\n                predicates.first()\n            {\n                Some(Binder { value: trait_ref.clone(), bound_vars: bound_vars.clone() })\n            } else {\n                None\n            }\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the type of `ty[i]` for builtin types.\n    pub fn builtin_index(&self) -> Option<Ty> {\n        match self.rigid()? {\n            RigidTy::Array(ty, _) | RigidTy::Slice(ty) => Some(*ty),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the type and mutability of `*ty` for builtin types.\n    ///\n    /// The parameter `explicit` indicates if this is an *explicit* dereference.\n    /// Some types -- notably raw ptrs -- can only be dereferenced explicitly.\n    pub fn builtin_deref(&self, explicit: bool) -> Option<TypeAndMut> {\n        match self.rigid()? {\n            RigidTy::Adt(def, args) if def.is_box() => {\n                Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *args.0.first()?.ty()?, mutability: Mutability::Not })\n            }\n            RigidTy::Ref(_, ty, mutability) => {\n                Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *ty, mutability: *mutability })\n            }\n            RigidTy::RawPtr(ty, mutability) if explicit => {\n                Some(TypeAndMut { ty: *ty, mutability: *mutability })\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the function signature for function like types (Fn, FnPtr, and Closure)\n    pub fn fn_sig(&self) -> Option<PolyFnSig> {\n        match self {\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.fn_sig(*def, args))),\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnPtr(sig)) => Some(sig.clone()),\n            TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::Closure(_def, args)) => Some(with(|cx| cx.closure_sig(args))),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the discriminant type for this type.\n    pub fn discriminant_ty(&self) -> Option<Ty> {\n        self.rigid().map(|ty| with(|cx| cx.rigid_ty_discriminant_ty(ty)))\n    }\n\n    /// Deconstruct a function type if this is one.\n    pub fn fn_def(&self) -> Option<(FnDef, &GenericArgs)> {\n        if let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(def, args)) = self {\n            Some((*def, args))\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n}\n\npub struct TypeAndMut {\n    pub ty: Ty,\n    pub mutability: Mutability,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum RigidTy {\n    Bool,\n    Char,\n    Int(IntTy),\n    Uint(UintTy),\n    Float(FloatTy),\n    Adt(AdtDef, GenericArgs),\n    Foreign(ForeignDef),\n    Str,\n    Array(Ty, TyConst),\n    Pat(Ty, Pattern),\n    Slice(Ty),\n    RawPtr(Ty, Mutability),\n    Ref(Region, Ty, Mutability),\n    FnDef(FnDef, GenericArgs),\n    FnPtr(PolyFnSig),\n    Closure(ClosureDef, GenericArgs),\n    Coroutine(CoroutineDef, GenericArgs),\n    CoroutineClosure(CoroutineClosureDef, GenericArgs),\n    Dynamic(Vec<Binder<ExistentialPredicate>>, Region),\n    Never,\n    Tuple(Vec<Ty>),\n    CoroutineWitness(CoroutineWitnessDef, GenericArgs),\n}\n\nimpl RigidTy {\n    /// Get the discriminant type for this type.\n    pub fn discriminant_ty(&self) -> Ty {\n        with(|cx| cx.rigid_ty_discriminant_ty(self))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From<RigidTy> for TyKind {\n    fn from(value: RigidTy) -> Self {\n        TyKind::RigidTy(value)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy,       IntTy::Isize => MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes(),\n            IntTy::I8 => 1,\n            IntTy::I16 => 2,\n            IntTy::I32 => 4,\n            IntTy::I64 => 8,\n {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub AdtDef;\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum AdtKind {\n    Enum,\n    Union,\n    Struct,\n}\n\nimpl AdtDef {\n    pub fn cx.adt_variants_len(*self))\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve the variants in this ADT.\n    pub fn variants(&self) -> Vec<VariantDef> {\n        self.variants_iter().collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Iterate over the variants in this ADT.\n    pub fn variants_iter(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = VariantDef> {\n        (0..self.num_variants())\n            .map(|idx| VariantDef { idx: VariantIdx::to_val(idx), adt_def: *self })\n    }\n\n    pub fn variant(&self, idx: VariantIdx) -> Option<VariantDef> {\n        (idx.to_index() < self.num_variants()).then_some(VariantDef { idx, adt_def: *self })\n    }\n\n    pub fn repr(&self) -> ReprOptions {\n        with(|cx| cx.adt_repr(*self))\n    }\n\n    pub fn discriminant_for_variant(&self, idx: VariantIdx) -> Discr {\n        with(|cx| cx.adt_discr_for_variant(*self, idx))\n    }\n}\n\npub struct Discr {\n    pub val: u128,\n    pub ty: Ty,\n}\n\n/// Definition of a variant, which can be either a struct / union field or an enum variant.\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct VariantDef {\n    /// The variant index.\n    ///\n    /// ## Warning\n    /// Do not access this field directly!\n    pub idx: VariantIdx,\n    /// The data type where this variant comes from.\n    /// For now, we use this to retrieve information about the variant itself so we don't need to\n    /// cache more information.\n    ///\n    /// ## Warning\n    /// Do not access this field directly!\n    pub adt_def: AdtDef,\n}\n\nimpl VariantDef {\n    pub fn name(&self) -> Symbol {\n        with(|cx| cx.variant_name(*self))\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve all the fields in this variant.\n    // We expect user to cache this and use it directly since today it is expensive to generate all\n    // fields name.\n    pub fn fields(&self) -> Vec<FieldDef> {\n        with(|cx| cx.variant_fields(*self))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct FieldDef {\n    /// The field definition.\n    ///\n    /// ## Warning\n    /// Do not access this field directly! This is public for the compiler to have access to it.\n    pub def: DefId,\n\n    /// The field name.\n    pub name: Symbol,\n}\n\nimpl FieldDef {\n    /// Retrieve the type of this field instantiating and normalizing it with the given arguments.\n    ///\n    /// This will assume the type can be instantiated with these arguments.\n    pub fn ty_with_args(&self, args: &GenericArgs) -> Ty {\n        with(|cx| cx.def_ty_with_args(self.def, args))\n    }\n\n    /// Retrieve the type of this field.\n    pub fn ty(&self) -> Ty {\n        with(|cx| cx.def_ty(self.def))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Display for AdtKind {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        f.write_str(match self {\n            AdtKind::Enum => \"enum\",\n            AdtKind::Union => \"union\",\n            AdtKind::Struct => \"struct\",\n        })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AdtKind {\n    pub fn is_enum(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, AdtKind::Enum)\n    }\n\n    pub fn is_struct(&self) -> AliasDef;\n}\n\ncrate_def! {\n    /// A trait's definition.\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub TraitDef;\n}\n\nimpl_crate_def_items! {\n    TraitDef;\n}\n\nimpl TraitDef {\n    pub fn pub RegionDef;\n}\n\ncrate_def! {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    pub CoroutineWitnessDef;\n}\n\n/// A list of generic arguments.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Hash, Serialize)]\npub struct GenericArgs(pub Vec<GenericArgKind>);\n\nimpl std::ops::Index<ParamTy> for GenericArgs {\n    type Output = Ty;\n\n    fn index(&self, index: ParamTy) -> &Self::Output {\n        self.0[index.index as usize].expect_ty()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::ops::Index<ParamConst> for GenericArgs {\n    type Output = TyConst;\n\n    fn index(&self, index: ParamConst) -> &Self::Output {\n        self.0[index.index GenericArgKind {\n    /// Panic if this generic argument is not a type, otherwise\n    /// return the type.\n    #[track_caller]\n    pub fn expect_ty(&self) -> &Ty {\n        match self {\n            GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => ty,\n            _ => panic!(\"{self:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Panic if this generic argument is not a const, otherwise\n    /// return the const.\n    #[track_caller]\n    pub fn expect_const(&self) -> &TyConst {\n        match self {\n            GenericArgKind::Const(c) => c,\n            _ => panic!(\"{self:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub enum TermKind {\n    Type(Ty),\n    Const(TyConst),\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub Ty {\n        self.inputs_and_output[self.inputs_and_output.len() - 1]\n    }\n\n    pub fn inputs(&self) -> &[Ty] {\n        &self.inputs_and_output[..self.inputs_and_output.len() - 1]\n Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct Binder<T> {\n    pub value: T,\n    pub bound_vars: Vec<BoundVariableKind>,\n}\n\nimpl<T> Binder<T> {\n    /// Create a new binder with the given bound vars.\n dummy(value: T) -> Self {\n        Binder { value, bound_vars: vec![] }\n    }\n\n    pub fn skip_binder(self) -> T {\n        self.value\n    }\n\n    pub fn map_bound_ref<F, U>(&self, f: F) -> new_value = f(value);\n        Binder { value: new_value, bound_vars }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize)]\npub struct EarlyBinder<T> {\n    pub value: Allocation length is `{}`, but requested range `{:?}`\",\n                self.bytes.len(),\n                range\n            ));\n        }\n        let raw = self.bytes[range]\n            .iter()\n            .copied()\n            .collect::<Option<Vec<_>>>()\n            .ok_or_else(|| error!(\"Found uninitialized bytes: `{:?}`\", self.bytes))?;\n        read_target_uint(&raw)\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to an unassigned integer.\n    pub fn read_uint(&self) -> Result<u128, Error> {\n        if self.bytes.len() > 16 {\n            return Err(error!(\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\"));\n        }\n        let raw = self.raw_bytes()?;\n        read_target_uint(&raw)\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to a signed integer.\n    pub fn read_int(&self) -> Result<i128, Error> {\n        if self.bytes.len() > 16 {\n            return Err(error!(\"Allocation is bigger than largest integer\"));\n        }\n        let raw = self.raw_bytes()?;\n        read_target_int(&raw)\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation and try to convert it to a boolean.\n    pub fn read_bool(&self) -> Result<bool, Error> {\n        match self.read_int()? {\n            0 => Ok(false),\n            1 => Ok(true),\n            val => Err(error!(\"Unexpected value for bool: `{val}`\")),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Read this allocation as a pointer and return whether it represents a `null` pointer.\n    pub fn is_null(&self) -> Result<bool, Error> {\n        let len = self.bytes.len();\n        let ptr_len = MachineInfo::target_pointer_width().bytes();\n        if len != ptr_len {\n            return Err(error!(\"Expected width of pointer (`{ptr_len}`), but found: `{len}`\"));\n        }\n
